Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink may generate incorrect code for models with Stateflow exported functions
Description: When generating code for a model with Stateflow exported functions and Fixed-Point types, the generated code may be incorrect. This bug may occur when a Stateflow exported function returns a Fixed-Point type or the function is used in conjunction with Fixed-Point operations. One possible way to determine the occurrence of this bug is to compare simulation results to code generation results.  The following MathWorks article explains how to do this comparison: How can I compare results from my Simulink model to those obtained using the code generation technology from the same model?   The MathWorks strongly recommends that you upgrade to a newer release.

Summary: MATLAB crash when generating code for models with Stateflow exported functions
Description: Generating code for a model with Stateflow exported functions with fixed-point return type may cause MATLAB to crash. The occurrence of this bug will print out the following in the MATLAB command prompt: CGIR assertion 'error: FixptType::createFxpDtpProp: failed with unknown constructor or wrong number of bits.' failed in '.\base_core\type\FixptType.cpp:323'

Description: MATLAB can sporadically crash with a stack trace pointing to sf_sfun.mex:update_default_port_size , similar to the following:   [0] sf_sfun.mexw32:void __cdecl update_default_port_size(struct SimStruct_tag *,class CDR_ChartCompiledInfo *,int,bool)(0x1d978ff0, 1, 0x22f9af00, 0x1d978ff0) + 128 bytes  [1] sf_sfun.mexw32:void __cdecl set_port_size_info(struct SimStruct_tag *,class CDR_ChartCompiledInfo *,int,bool,struct DimsInfo_tag const *)(0x1d978ff0, 0x22f9af00, 0, 1) + 176 bytes  [2] sf_sfun.mexw32:void __cdecl mdlSetInputPortDimensionInfo(struct SimStruct_tag *,int,struct DimsInfo_tag const *)(0x1d978ff0, 0, 0x317e1040, 0x1f2c4158) + 77 bytes  [3] sf_sfun.mexw32:_mexFunction(0xfffffffe, 0x30d55e04, 4, 0x30d6be58) + 312 bytes

Summary: Error when using parameters and Stateflow charts in same library
Description: If you have a library model that uses parameters (either mask parameters or parameter data objects) and also contains Stateflow charts from a reference model, Stateflow can throw the following error message when you update the diagram or simulate a model that uses this library: Error using Stateflow.SLINSF.SubchartMan.clearMaskParams This error can appear even if you do not have user-defined masks in your library or top model.

Summary: Abnormal exit when running Simulink Code Inspector compatibility check Check usage of Ports and Subsystems blocks
Description: If you check code inspection compatibility for a model with an unconnected block input port,  Simulink Code Inspector compatibility check Check usage of Ports and Subsystems blocks  exits abnormally.  The compatibilty report states Abnormal exit: Error due to multiple causes .
Workaround: In your model or subsystem, connect all block input ports. To check for unconnected objects in your model, run compatibility check Check for unconnected objects in the model.

Summary: Set Overload Counter Block does not work correctly for multitasking models.
Description: Set Overload Counter Block does not set the overload counter correctly for multitasking models.
Workaround: Update to version R2013a or to a later version. For users who cannot update to R2013a, please contact MathWorks technical support for a R2010b patch.
Products: xPC Target Embedded Option, Simulink Real-Time

Summary: Opening custom libraries that contain physical modeling blocks sometimes do not load or only partially load
Description: Custom libraries that contain physical modeling blocks may fail to load, or fail to load completely. Physical modeling blocks are included in products such as SimScape and SimMechanics. Two different behaviors are possible: When opening the model, an error similar to the following is reported in the command window: Assertion failed: Deviant '2': object of typ SLM3I.Port' [id=-1] is not present at b:\matlab\src\m3i\bundles\../kernel/deviant/Deviant.cpp:68:   The model opens, but one or more blocks does not appear.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generator produces incorrect C++ code when applying code replacements for functions with pointer types
Description: When all the following conditions exist, the code generator produces incorrect code: Model was configured for C++ code generation and for the code generator to apply a code replacement library (CRL). CRL specified code replacement for a function with an argument that has a pointer type.  Under these conditions, the generated code passes the replacement function a pointer to the expected pointer instead of the pointer. The incorrect code causes build errors. Example: C replacement function prototype: void myReplacementFcn(double *x); Assumption: real32_T in1[3]; Expected code replacement: myReplacementFcn(*(real32_T (*)[3]) in1);  Incorrect code generated due to this bug: myReplacementFcn(*(real32_T (*)[3]) &in1);

Summary: ncread returns incorrect data when reading from certain NetCDF files located on remote servers using the OPeNDAP protocol
Description: For certain NetCDF files located on OPeNDAP servers, ncread  returns incorrect data when reading an entire variable. This data does not match the data that ncread  reads from a subset of the variable. For example, the code below displays Not Equal  for certain NetCDF files located on OPeNDAP servers. urlStr = 'http://myserver:8080/thredds/dodsC/myLocation/myfile.nc'; varName = 'myVar'; allData = ncread(urlStr, varName, [1 1 1 1], [Inf Inf 1 Inf]); partData = ncread(urlStr, varName, [1 1 1 1], [Inf Inf 1 9]); if isequal(allData(:, :, 1, 1:9), partData)     disp('Equal'); else     disp('Not Equal'); end
Workaround: Read the entire variable using multiple calls to ncread, where each call reads a subset of the data as shown in the example below.% This file contains a variable called myVar that has four dimensions% lat, lon, depth, and time, and has extents 351-by-391-by-12-by-173. The code% below attempts to read all the data in the variable for depth == 1.urlStr = 'http://myserver:8080/thredds/dodsC/myLocation/myfile.nc';varName = 'myVar';info = ncinfo(urlStr, varName);fixedDepth = 1;actData = zeros([info.Size(1) info.Size(2) 1 info.Size(4)], info.Datatype);countToRead = 9; % Arbitrarily chosen.startLoc = 1;while startLoc <= info.Size(4)    tempData = ncread(urlStr, varName, [1 1 fixedDepth startLoc], [Inf Inf fixedDepth, countToRead]);    actData(:, :, 1, startLoc:startLoc+countToRead-1) = tempData;        startLoc = startLoc+countToRead;    if startLoc >= info.Size(4)        countToRead = info.Size(4) - startLoc + 1;    endend

Summary: Configuration sets saved in R2012a with ModelDataLogs signal logging format have Dataset format when opened in R2012b
Description: In R2012a, a configuration set (Simulink.ConfigSet ) is given a logging format of ModelDataLogs  and saved to a MAT-file. In R2012b, the MAT-file is loaded and the configuration set is changed to Dataset  format.
Workaround: Download the attached ConfigSetWrapper.m file.You can use the MATLAB class, ConfigSetWrapper, which is defined in this file, to store a configuration set with the correct signal logging format created in R2012a and subsequently retrieve the configuration set with the correct format in R2012b, as described below.In R2012a:1. Store the correct signal logging format for a given Simulink.Configset, for example, sampleCfg:wrapped_SampleCfg = ConfigSetWrapper(sampleCfg);2. Save the wrapped configuration set in a MAT-file:save matfile_12a_wrapper wrapped_SampleCfgThen, in R2012b: 1. Retrieve the correct format:load matfile_12a_wrapper 2. Create a Configset using the configset you wrapped in R2012a: sampleCfg = wrapped_SampleCfg.ConfigSet; 3. Ensure that the configuration set sampleCfg now has the correct signal logging format:get_param(sampleCfg, 'SignalLoggingSaveFormat')

Summary: When the xPC Target option "Double buffer parameter changes" is selected, setting a Fixed Point parameter can reset the parameter value to zero.
Description: When the xPC Target option "Double buffer parameter changes" is selected, setting a parameter of Fixed Point datatype resets the parameter value to zero in the target application.
Workaround: As a workaround, clear the xPC Target option "Double buffer parameter changes".

Summary: Package and storage class not preserved when copying blocks with state
Description: You specify the state storage class of blocks with discrete states using two parameters: Package  and Code generation storage class . When you copy blocks with discrete states, Simulink does not preserve these two parameters, if Package  is set to a value other than --- None --- . In addition, Simulink incorrectly stores state data in the code generated for a selected subsystem, when both these conditions are met. The Package  parameter of any block in that subsystem is set to a value other than --- None --- . You generate code for the subsystem by selecting it in the Simulink Editor and clicking Code > C/C++ Code > Build Selected Subsystem .

Summary: Size mismatches from back propagation sometimes cause assertions or stack traces
Description: The following circumstances can cause a stack trace or assertion:An output of a MATLAB function block or a Stateflow chart with MATLAB as the action language is left as inherited. The size of the output is inferred from a definition in the MATLAB function block or the Stateflow chart. This inferred size is different from the size due to back propagation. The back propagated size is determined if a block connected to the output of the MATLAB function block or Stateflow chart specifies the size instead of leaving it as inherited.  There can be a stack trace or assertion produced on the command line instead of a helpful error message. A symptom of this includes messages such asUnexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR: .\util_cg\cg_sanity_checker.cpp line 1412: Failed assertion: PointerType::isa(aExpr->lhs()->type())  orCodegen Assertion 'typeReplaced.getMatrixSize() == typeReplacing.getMatrixSize()' failed in 'b:\matlab\src\rtwcg\rtwcg_utils\rtwcg_dataconnector.cpp:656

Summary: Belt Drive block incorrectly applies belt pre-tension
Description: The Belt Drive block incorrectly applies the Pre-tension parameter. This issue stems from a Translational Spring block in the underlying Simscape block diagram. You can see a schematic of this diagram in the Belt Drive block reference page. In the block diagram, two parallel branches with Translational Spring and Translational Damper blocks connect two Belt Pulley blocks. Each branch in the block diagram models compliance in one branch of the drive belt.  The Translational Spring block within each branch applies the Pre-tension value that you specify to the corresponding drive belt branch. The block does this by setting the initial spring deformation based on your pre-tension value.  In the Translational Spring block that connects to port B of pulley A, the initial deformation parameter is incorrect. This can lower friction below its intended value, increasing the likelihood of belt slip. The effect is maximal at low rotational velocities and large Pre-tension values.
Workaround: Manually model a belt drive using Belt Pulley, Translational Spring, and Translational Damper blocks. A schematic in the Belt Drive block reference page shows how to connect the blocks in the open configuration. For the crossed configuration, switch the A and B ports of the Belt Pulley B block.In the Translational Spring block dialog boxes, specify the Initial deformation parameter. The correct value for this parameter is the fraction (Pre-tension/Longitudinal stiffness).To increase numerical stability in the presence of rapidly changing system inputs, consider attaching small masses to the belt.

Summary: Wrong copy method might be called when using matlab.mixin.Copyable with multiple inheritance.
Description: Given a subclass with multiple superclasses, one of which is matlab.mixin.Copyable  (which defines a public sealed method named copy ) , and another superclass that defines a public unsealed method named copy , MATLAB might invoke the wrong copy  method when calling copy  with an instance of the subclass.  When two superclasses define public methods with the same name, and one of the superclasses defines the method as sealed, then the subclass adopts the sealed version.  Therefore, in this situation the subclass should adopt the copy   method from matlab.mixin.Copyable . However, due to a bug, the unsealed copy   method from the second superclass might be adopted by the subclass instead.

Summary: MWArray javadoc missing from MATLAB Builder JA R2012b product
Description: The MWArray  javadoc is missing from the MATLAB Builder JA product due to a incomplete conversion activity. As a result, users of the MATLAB Builder JA product have no MWArray  API javadoc in the R2012b product.
Workaround: Users can reference the javadoc zipped and attached to this EBR as a workaround.The problem has been solved going forward and the javadoc will appear in the R2013a build.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier fails to return derived ranges in design error detection mode on certain Windows machines
Description: On certain Windows machines, Simulink Design Verifier fails to return derived ranges in design error detection mode. Deriving ranges in Simulink Design Verifier relies on Microsoft Visual C++ redistributable binaries. Installing these binaries will resolve the issue.
Workaround: Install both the Microsoft Visual C++ 2005 and 2008 redistributable packages following Microsoft's instructions at the links referenced below.Visual C++ 2005 SP1 redistributable package: http://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=5638Visual C++ 2008 redistributable package:http://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=29

Summary: Derived range analysis fails on Windows computers with inadequate installation
Description: On Windows computers with inadequate installation, derived range analysis fails to return any results. The derived range analysis process relies on Microsoft Visual C++ redistributable binaries. Due to a bug in the MathWorks installer, the necessary redistributable binaries are not automatically installed. If the redistributable binaries are installed on the computer by other means, then the derived range analysis will work properly.

Summary: Editing the mask of a Subsystem block using the Mask Editor may result in a segmentation violation if it already contains promoted parameters.
Description: For the problem to happen all the following conditions must be true: The block should be a masked Subsystem block. Mask must be edited using the Mask Editor. Mask has parameters that are promoted from masked child blocks.
Workaround: As a workaround use the mask object for editing the mask. To get the mask object, use:aMaskObject = Simulink.Mask.get(blockPath);

Summary: Referenced models with custom targets that disable parameters may cause the model reference SIM target to not build
Description: If you have a referenced model with a custom target that disables parameters (e.g., EfficientMapNaN2IntZero ), Simulink may Not build the model reference SIM target for that model and Give an error such as:         Changing property 'EfficientFloat2IntCast' is not allowed
Workaround: To avoid this problem, do one of the following:Edit the custom target so that it does not disable the parameter.Reference the model in Normal mode.

Summary: Incorrect error message for reusable library subsystem with compact file packaging
Description: A model generates an incorrect error when it contains a reusable subsystem with the following configuration: The reusable subsystem is linked to a library On the Subsystem Block Parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation  tab, Function name options  and File name options  are both set to Auto  On the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation > Code Placement  pane, File packaging format  is set to Compact   The error message states that the reusable subsystem parameter, File name options , must be set to Auto . This error message is generated even though the subsystem has File name options  set to Auto .
Workaround: To avoid the error message, do any of the following:Change the File packaging format to Modular.Disable the library link in the model.Change the reusable subsystem parameter, Function name options, to a value other than Auto.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated C++ code is incorrect for models with nested subsystems that generate reusable functions?
Description: Simulink? Coder? and Embedded Coder? might generate incorrect code for a model, if all of these conditions are met: Model configuration parameter?Language  is set to C++  or C++ (Encapsulated ). Model?contains at least one non-virtual subsystem nested within another non-virtual subsystem. Summary: When using Accelerator mode to simulate a model that uses root-level inports to load variable-size signals, Simulink may log incorrect signal dimensions when simulation time exceeds the range of input data
Description: When using Accelerator mode to simulate a model that uses root-level inports to load variable-size signals, Simulink may log incorrect signal dimensions when simulation time exceeds the range of input data. In this case, the model uses the signal dimensions of the last input data point but should use the same dimensions as those used for Normal mode. The actual signal values and logged signal values are correct and unaffected by this bug.
Workaround: As a workaround, download, unzip, and run the attached script on your model. If the script detects an issue with your model, consider one of the following actions:Run the model in Normal mode instead of Accelerator mode. Note, this workaround may impact performance.Extend the time span of input data beyond the simulation stop time. Note, this workaround requires you to edit the input data to the model.Set simulation stop time at the last time point present in the input data. Note, this workaround requires you to edit the model's stop time.

Summary: Wrong simulation results for some models with custom Simscape blocks in local solver mode
Description: When you simulate with the Use local solver  option selected, wrong simulation results are possible for a restricted class of models using custom Simscape blocks. Specifically, models whose equations are completely linear or affine in states, inputs, and time, except for one or more equations which are of the form x.der == c  in every conditional branch (with at least two different values of c  depending on the branch), are simulated incorrectly. Examples:  If the model consists of only one custom block (connected to the Solver Configuration block), with only one equation in its Simscape file, x.der == 1 , the model simulates correctly, because no conditional branching is involved.  If the file still has exactly one equation, but it involves a conditional: if x > 0 x.der == 1 else x.der == 2 end , simulation results are wrong.  If any nonlinear equations are present in the model, there is no bug.
Workaround: If you are using custom Simscape blocks and a local solver, and your model might be purely linear or affine in states, input and time, except for equations of the form described above, replace each x.der == c expression in the custom block equations with x.der == u, where u is an additional block input. Feed the input port with a PS Constant block with value c.

Summary: Incorrect simulation results for purely affine models with custom Simscape blocks
Description: If a Simscape model has a block whose .ssc file has one or more equations of the form m * x.der == c , for nonzero constant or parameter value c , and the model otherwise consists of purely linear equations which depend on states and inputs only, simulation results are incorrect, unless you use local solver.  Examples:  If a model consists of only one custom block (with an input connected to the Solver Configuration block), whose Simscape file has only one equation: x.der == 1 , the output for x  will be a flat line (that is, simulating x.der == 0 ).  Similarly, if the block has two equations: x1.der == 1  and x1 + x2 == u  (states x , input u ), the same bug occurs for x1 , because the whole model consists of linear equations and equations of the form x.der == c .  However, for these equations: x1.der == 1  and x1 * x2 == u  , there is no bug, because the second equation is nonlinear.
Workaround: If you see a flat line in simulation output (or for any state variable, which can be checked using Simscape data logging) and there is a possibility that your whole model is purely linear except for equations of the form x.der == c, replace these equations with x.der == u, where u is an input to the block, and feed that input port with a PS Constant block whose value is set to c.

Summary: Closing a code coverage report for a model with Stateflow content can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: If you generate a code coverage report is and then close that report while a Simulink Editor or Stateflow Editor window or tab is displaying a Stateflow portion of a model, MATLAB may crash.
Workaround: Before generating a code coverage report for a model that has Stateflow content, close all editor windows that display the Stateflow content.

Summary: Code generation error for multirate subsystem with Custom Code block
Description: Building a model in which a multirate subsystem contains a Custom Code block might fail if all of the following conditions are true: In the model, an atomic subsystem contains a multirate subsystem, which in turn contains a Custom Code block. Neither the outer atomic subsystem nor the inner multirate subsystem contain a block that runs at a rate equal to the greatest common divisor (gcd ) of the rates in the multirate subsystem. For example, the subsystems might contain blocks that have sample time periods of 2 and 3, but none that have a sample time period of 1.  Under these conditions, building the model might fail with an error similar to the following: Code generation assertion 'taskIdx >= 0 && taskIdx < getNumTasks()' failed in '.\rtwcg_utils\rtwcg_fcnconst.cpp:646'.
Workaround: To work around this bug, open the subsystem that contains the Custom Code block and insert a block that has a sample time equal to the gcd of the rates in the subsystem.

Description: The help documentation for the kmlwrite  function contains incorrect instructions for launching Google Earth on Macintosh from within MATLAB? .  The help says, for Unix and Macintosh users, display the KML file with:  cmd = 'googleearth '; system([cmd filename]);

Summary: MATLAB Desktop stops responding after changing to a folder containing an MDL file with a Description field containing only new-line characters
Description: If you have an MDL file with a Description field containing only new-line characters, and you change folder to view the model in the Current Folder browser, the MATLAB Desktop stops responding and you might see exceptions in the Command Window.The exception trace that is sometimes shown in the command window begins like this:Exception in thread "FileDecorationCache request queue" java.util.NoSuchElementExceptionat java.util.StringTokenizer.nextToken(Unknown Source)at com.mathworks.toolbox.simulink.desktopintegration.SimulinkFileInfoProvider$3.convert
Workaround: To avoid this problem, either close the Current Folder Browser before changing to a folder containing an MDL file which has a Description field containing only newline characters, or edit the model in Simulink to give it a different description.You can use the following code to help you find models with a Description field containing only newline characters:folder = 'C:\mymodels'; % use your folder name herefiles = dir(fullfile(folder,'*.mdl'));for i=1:numel(files)    info = Simulink.MDLInfo(files(i).name);    if ~isempty(info.Description) && all(info.Description==char(10))        fprintf('MDL file has description containing only new-line characters: %s\n',files(i).name);    endend

Description: The phased.ArrayGain  system object returns an incorrect value for SNR gain when applied to a subarray.

Summary: Compilation error when Simulink.Parameter object is used as the InitialValue of a Simulink.Signal object
Description: A compilation error results when a Simulink.Signal  object used in the model has: InitialValue property set to a Simulink.Parameter  object DataType propery set to a Simulink.AliasType  object  The following error message appears: Data type of InitialValue 'my-initial-value ' must be double or the same as the signal object data type

Summary: Error message Fatal error while parsing RVW summary version 1.9 when downloading verification results
Description: In the Polyspace verification environment, when you download results using the Polyspace Metrics Web interface, you might see the following error: Fatal error while parsing RVW summary version 1.9
Workaround: Installation instructions for the Polyspace R2012b client and serverLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your Polyspace installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your Polyspace installation. By default this is in c:\Polyspace or in /usr/local/Polyspace on Linux systems. This folder is referred to as <POLYSPACE> in subsequent steps. Polyspace_Common and PolyspaceFor<lang>_R2012b are the two principal sub-folders of <POLYSPACE>.Close the Polyspace verification environment and stop Polyspace queue manager service.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <POLYSPACE> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell:unzip -l <POLYSPACE>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <POLYSPACE> folder.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell: cd <POLYSPACE>unzip <.zip file>Check that binaries have appropriate ˇ®read & executeˇŻ permissions.Delete the .zip file in the <POLYSPACE> folder.Restart Polyspace queue manager service and reopen the Polyspace verification environment.

Summary: External Force and Torque block generates incorrect motion
Description: Using the External Force and Torque  block to apply a force or torque can produce incorrect motion. This error can occur if the External Force and Torque  block connects to a Rigid Transform  network and the force/torque resolution frame is the attached frame. Using the World  frame as the resolution frame eliminates the error.
Workaround: To prevent this error from occurring, insert a Weld Joint block between the External Force and Torque block and the frame to which it connects. For example, if a model contains the connection:|External Force and Torque|F-----Force/Torque Application Frame...insert the Weld Joint block as shown:|External Force and Torque|F-----B|Weld Joint|F-----Force/Torque Application Frame|Block|B refers to the base port of Block and |Block|F refers to the follower port of Block. The Weld Joint block does not affect model dynamics, since in both configurations, the External Force and Torque block rigidly connects to the force/torque application frame.If you use the World frame as the force/torque resolution frame, no workaround is required.?

Summary: When regenerating the qualification report using the SDD capability of Simulink Report Generator?, unlabeled Stateflow? transitions names in 'Requirements Traceability' might differ from the transition names provided in reference report.
Description: When the System Design Description (SDD) report capability of Simulink Report Generator generates requirements traceability information for a labeled Stateflow transition, it uses the transition label as the name of the transition. For example,[fails>=5].  However, in the case of unlabeled transitions, SDD uses a string with the word "Transition" followed by an ID. The ID is the value of the ID property of the Stateflow transition object (Stateflow.Transition ). Since the value of the ID property for a transition object changes every time the model is opened, regenerating the report for same model might lead to different transition names. The transition names might differ from those provided in the reference report.
Workaround: When reviewing the qualification reports, the Stateflow IDs for unlabeled transitions might differ from those provided in the reference reports. However, the reports accurately generate traceability information, even for models using unlabeled Stateflow transitions.There is no work around.

Summary: Object array expansion can cause memory leaks.
Description: In certain cases, expanding an object array without assigning specific values to all elements of the array can cause memory leaks. This can happen when the expanded array is also involved in some circular data structure, like a circular linked list. The following code sample causes objects of class myHandleClass  to leak memory. In the code, myHandleClass  is a handle class that has a property called next . a = myHandleClass;a(10) = myHandleClass; % array expansion happens hereb = myHandleClass;for i=1:10  a(i).next = b;endb.next = a; % make the list circulara = [];  Executing the above code followed by clear classes  returns the following warning message, which indicates objects of myHandleClass  have leaked. Warning: Objects of 'myHandleClass' class exist. Cannot clear this class or any of its superclasses.
Workaround: The workaround is to assign each element of the array with a specific value. For the sample code above. replace:a(10) = myHandleClass;withfor i=1:10 a(i) = myHandleClass;end

Summary: sign function issues an error message when the input belongs to a MATLAB integer class
Description: The sign  function issues an error and fails to compile when the input is int32 , int16 , int8 , uint32 , uint16 , or uint8 .

Summary: Simulation Data Inspector run comparison might fail for logged signals from scope
Description: Simulation Data Inspector might compare unrelated signals when data is in Array  format. In the Simulation Data Inspector, on the Compare Runs tab, the signal is shown with an error.

Summary: Upgrade Advisor fails to analyze the complete model hierarchy if files are renamed to SLX during upgrade process
Description: When using the Upgrade Advisor check to "Analyze model hierarchy for upgrade issues", the Advisor can fail to go through the complete model hierarchy. This problem occurs after you click Fix for the check "Check that the model is saved is SLX format". The Upgrade Advisor changes the model from MDL to SLX format. After this, when you click the "Analyze next model" button in the "Analyze model hierarchy for upgrade issues" check, the Advisor incorrectly reports that the next model to be analyzed has no child models. The problem is caused by the change in file name compared to the original analysis of model references.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, either do not save as SLX during hierarchy analysis for upgrade issues, or install the attached patch.1. Do not use the Upgrade Advisor to upgrade your models from MDL to SLX format. Alternatively you could use Simulink Project checks to upgrade models to SLX.?2. Install the patch, instructions below. After you install the patch, the Upgrade Advisor correctly continues to analyze model hierarchy after upgrading a model to SLX.Installation instructions       Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.       Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.       Exit MATLAB. Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder. Do this by navigating to  WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting  Run as administrator.    From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file. Summary: Functions that take only cell arrays as arguments do not work with BATCH
Description: Calling batch() with a function handle as the first argument, and specifying only cell arrays as input arguments, fails with the error "Batch cannot be used to run multiple scripts or functions."
Workaround: To run a function on a cluster that takes only cell arrays as arguments, use the explicit job and task programming interface.For example, if the function myFunction takes a single cell array x as an input argument, and has no output arguments, you can submit this to the cluster myCluster with the following code: >> job = createJob(myCluster); >> createTask(job,@myFunction,0,{{x}}); >> submit(job);

Summary: Models containing bus inputs to Rate Transition blocks with certain combinations of sample times can cause MATLAB to stop responding
Description: If a model contains bus inputs for a Rate Transition block, for certain combinations of rates on the input and output port of the Rate Transition block, MATLAB can stop responding when Simulink sets sample time properties for that block. This happens when the sample time GCD (Greatest Common Divisor) of the input and output sample times is replaced by another sample time that is the same theoretically but not bit-for-bit. Example:  A Rate Transition Block with an input sample time of [1,0] and an output sample time of [1,0.6] has a sample time GCD of 0.19999999999999998. If there are blocks elsewhere in the model which have a sample time of 0.2, this will be represented as 0.20000000000000001 and taken to be the sample time kept in the model. Since the GCD sample time (0.19999999999999998) is not in the model's list of sample times, MATLAB stops responding when Simulink sets the sample time properties of the Rate Transition block this way.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, perform the following steps:Replace the Rate Transition block with a subsystem.Within the subsystem, break the bus apart into its constituent signals.Put each signal through a Rate Transition block.Reconstruct the bus

Summary: Code inspection Failed to verify status for compatible models containing Discrete-Time Integrator blocks
Description: If your compatible model contains a Discrete-Time Ingegrator block inside a conditional subsystem, code inspection can result in a Failed to verify  status.
Workaround: Consider reconfiguring your model to avoid having a Discrete-Time Integrator block inside a conditional subsystem.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generation for custom blocks fails if variable-size parameters are used outside of tablelookup function
Description: Currently, you can use variable-size parameters only inside the tablelookup  function, as stated in documentation.   If you declare a parameter as variable-sized and then assign a scalar value to it and use it outside the tablelookup  function, code generation for the custom block fails with the following error messages: - With Windows compilers:  1. error C2065: 'ds' : undeclared identifier  2. error C2440: '=' : cannot convert from 'double' to 'const real_T *'  3. error C2198: 'f' : too few arguments for call  - With gcc compilers:  1. error: 'ds' undeclared (first use in this function)  2. error: incompatible types when assigning to type 'const real_T *' from type 'double'  3. error: too few arguments to function 'f'
Workaround: If code generation for a custom block fails with the error messages similar to listed above, look for a variable-size parameter used outside of tablelookup function in either equations or setup section and change to a fixed-size parameter. Use variable-size parameters only inside the tablelookup function, as stated in documentation.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generation assertion for Stateflow? chart with unsigned loop index
Description: In a Stateflow chart that contains a for loop, if the loop is a countdown loop, and the loop index is declared as unsigned, the code generator throws an assertion.
Workaround: In the Stateflow chart, change the constant in the Initializer expression to a tunable parameter. This can be achieved by using a Stateflow data parameter as the Initializer expression. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Optimization > Signals and Parameters pane, click the Inline parameters Configure button and add this parameter to the Global (tunable) parameters table.

Summary: Root Outport block pass by value through model function prototype control
Description: The code generator might generate incorrect code when all of the following conditions are met: For code generation, a root Outport block is specified as pass by value through model function prototype control. In the generated code, the root Outport block can be used as a state if the pass by value specification is not in place.  For an example, see the attached model mFPCBug.slx  or mFPCBug.mdl  for R2011b or earlier releases. In the generated code, the variable for the root Outport block is incorrectly generated as a local instead of a global.
Workaround: Download the attached MATLAB script WorkaroundForBugReport890956.p.To enable the workaround, execute the script WorkaroundForBugReport890956(true).To disable the workaround, execute the script WorkaroundForBugReport890956(false).

Summary: Transform Sensor block can give incorrect linear acceleration measurements
Description: Under certain conditions, the Transform Sensor block outputs incorrect linear acceleration measurements. Measurements selected from the dialog box Acceleration  menu are affected. For the error to occur, all of these conditions must be present: The Transform Sensor block connects to the World frame via frame lines or Rigid Transform blocks. It does not matter which Transform Sensor frame port connects to the World frame. The SimMechanics component containing the Transform Sensor block also contains a Mechanism Configuration block with a non-zero gravity vector.
Workaround: To prevent measurement errors, do not connect the Transform Sensor block to the World frame via direct frame lines or Rigid Transform blocks. Instead, use the Weld Joint block as shown in the following example.Example:Consider a model containing a Transform Sensor block. In the original configuration, this block rigidly connects to the World Frame block as follows:|World frame|W-------B|Transform Sensor|F-----Note: Bold terms denote a block. Letters W, B, and F represent World, Base and Follower frame ports, respectively.To avoid a measurement error, insert a Weld Joint block between Transform Sensor and World Frame blocks:|World Frame|W-------B|Weld Joint|F-------B|Transform Sensor|F------The Weld Joint block does not affect model kinematics or dynamics. In the updated model, this block makes World Frame W and Transform Sensor B frames coincident for all time - the same relationship used in the original model.Note: Adding a Weld Joint block to the model can slow down simulation. In most cases, this effect is negligible.

Summary: Preprocessor macros not saved in configuration file
Description: When creating a new project, you might specify preprocessor directives through the Configuration > Target & Compiler > Macros  pane. However, when you reopen the project, you do not see the specified directives.
Workaround: On the Configuration > Target & Compiler > Macros pane, in the Preprocessor definitions section, click the Add row to table button. This action creates an empty line in the Macro table. Then, save the project (Ctrl+S).

Summary: Interpolation functions return incorrect results when extrapval = NaN and method = 'spline'
Description: interp2 , interp3 , and interpn  do not honor extrapval = NaN  when method = 'spline' . For example, [X,Y] = meshgrid(1:2,1:2);Z = rand(2);interp2(X,Y,Z,3,3,'spline',NaN)  returns a numeric value instead of NaN .

Summary: Code generation might fail for subsystem specified as Function with separate data
Description: If you select the Function with separate data  checkbox for a subsystem, a TLC error might occur during code generation of the model. The error message is similar to the following: Error: File: matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc Line: 1509 Column: 51 Unable to find ContStatesVarGroupIndex within the System scope
Workaround: To avoid the error, do one of the following:On the Subsystem block parameter dialog box, on the Code Generation tab, keep the Function packaging parameter set to Nonreusable function and clear the Function with separate data checkbox.Change the Function packaging parameter to Reusable function.On the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Optimization > Signals and Parameters pane, clear the Enable local block outputs checkbox.?

Summary: Consecutive delayed restarts of Time-Based Entity Generator block might cause a MATLAB crash
Description: If you run a SimEvents model when ALL of these conditions are true, MATLAB crashes: The model has a Time-Based Entity Generator block with Response when unblocked  set to Delayed restart . An entity generator is in a blocked state when a downstream condition prevents entity departure. When an event occurs to clear this downstream condition, the generator is unblocked. A blocked Time-Based Entity Generator block receives two or more consecutive unblocking events. The generator block receives the second and successive unblocking events while it is in the delayed restart state triggered by the first unblocking event.  The crash does not occur for models created in releases prior to R2011b, even if they are run in R2011b.
Workaround: To work around this issue, do ONE of the following:If possible, set the Time-Based Entity Generator Response when unblocked parameter to Immediate restart.If you must use Delayed restart in the Time-Based Entity Generator, reconfigure your model to ensure that it does not trigger a subsequent unblocking event while the generator block is in the delayed restart state triggered by the first unblocking event.

Summary: Generated code might not compile for model argument with structure field
Description: The generated code might not compile for a model, if: The model contains a Model block. The model passes a model argument to that Model block as a structure field.  For example, MyStruct  is a structure defined in the model or base workspace. The model passes a structure field to a Model block as MyStruct.field1 .

Summary: Validation of cdelta input in contourcmap is too strict
Description: When the optional cdelta  input to the contourcmap  function is a vector, it must consist of evenly spaced values. This condition is checked by the function, but the validation is too strict and may result in an errorˇŞeven when the input comes directly from the linspace  function.
Workaround: To work around this problem you can:Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to below as <MATLAB>.Make a back-up copy of the file <MATLAB>/toolbox/map/mapdisp/contourcmap.mExit MATLAB.Edit the file <MATLAB>/toolbox/map/mapdisp/contourcmap.m. (You may have to change file permissions first.)In subfunction computeColorLevels, replace the expression tolerance = eps with tolerance = 100*eps(max(abs(cdelta(:))))Restart MATLAB and run rehash toolbox at the command line.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect preprocessor conditionals for block with variants
Description: If you generate preprocessor conditionals for a model variant block or a variant subsystem block, then incorrect preprocessor conditionals may appear in the code when all of the following are true: The block does not combine its output and update. The parent subsystem of the block combines its output and updates. No other blocks contribute code between the variant block output and the update code.  The following code illustrates an example scenario; it calls the update function of Model_A in the same preprocessor block as the calls to the output and update functions of Model_A and Model_B: #if VARIANT_A/* ModelReference: '<Root>/Model Variants' */Model_A();#elif VARIANT_B/* ModelReference: '<Root>/Model Variants' */Model_B();#elif VARIANT_A/* Update for ModelReference: '<Root>/Model Variants' */Model_A_Update();#endif                                The correct code would place the call to the update function of Model_A into its own preprocessor block, as follows: #if VARIANT_A/* ModelReference: '<Root>/Model Variants' */Model_A();#elif VARIANT_B/* ModelReference: '<Root>/Model Variants' */Model_B();#endif                               #if VARIANT_A/* Update for ModelReference: '<Root>/Model Variants' */Model_A_Update();#endif
Workaround: In some cases, deselecting the configuration parameter "Code Generation > Interface > Code interface > Single output/update function" may work around the bug.

Summary: Editing the name of a subchart might remove state actions
Description: If you begin editing the name of a subchart at the chart level (from outside the subchart), the state actions inside the subchart might disappear. You do not need to actually change the name of the subchart for this issue to occur. Entering and exiting the name editing mode is all that's required. If you edit the name from inside the subchart, the state actions remain intact.
Workaround: Install a patch:Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file for your platform and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Code inspection Failed to verify status for compatible models containing Math Function or Divide blocks?
Description: If your compatible model contains either of the following blocks: Math Function with block parameter Function  set to reciprocal  Divide  and the denominator is a constant with a power of two (i.e., 2, 4, 8, 16,...), code inspection can result in a Failed to verify  status.
Workaround: Consider reconfiguring your model to avoid division by a power of two.

Summary: MATLAB asserts when generating code for Outport block in conditionally executed subsystem.
Description: MATLAB throws a CGIR assertion when generating code for a model with the following: The model has a conditionally executed subsystem whose Outport block is fed by a signal-copy Signal Conversion block The Signal Conversion block is fed by a Bus Creator block which has multiple inputs. On the Bus Creator parameters dialog box, the Output as nonvirtual bus  checkbox is not selected. On the Subsystem block parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation  tab, Function packaging  is not set to Reusable function.   One or more fields of the bus signal from the Outport block are not used in the model.  ?
Workaround: Do one of the following:On the Subsystem block parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation tab, change Function packaging to Reusable function.On the Bus Creator parameters dialog box, specify the Output data type as an explicitly defined bus data type and select the Output as nonvirtual bus checkbox. On the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Optimization pane, clear the Block reduction checkbox. This might increase code size significantly.

Summary: Generated code from model reference build fails to compile for model with nonreusable subsystem
Description: If you execute any one of the following functions: slbuild (model, 'ModelReferenceSimTarget')  slbuild (model, 'ModelReferenceRTWTarget')  Simulink.ModelReference.protect (___)   And If your model has all of the following conditions: Contains a Subsystem block which receives input from a Constant block. Has a Treat as atomic unit  check box selected or unavailable in the Subsystem block parameter dialog box. In the Subsystem Parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation  tab, you select Function packaging  as Nonreusable function .  Then Your generated code might not compile. ?
Workaround: Do one of the following:Change the Function packaging parameter to Inline or Reusable Function.Move the Constant block inside the atomic subsystem.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: comm.RSEncoder does not support codegen when BitInput property is set to true.
Description: When using codegen command to compile a matlab function containing comm.RSEncoder System object, with BitInput property set to true, will throw the following error: "Class mismatch (uint8 ~= embedded.fi {ufix1}). The class to the left is the class of the left-hand side of the assignment." Similar issue happens to comm.RSDecoder and similar workaround can be applied.
Workaround: Code gen works when BitInput property is set to false. The workaround is as follows: 1. Set BitInput property to false 2. Add a bit-to-integer conversion prior to encoding 3. Add an integer-to-bit conversion after encoding This will allow the code gen to work and obtain the same result.

Summary: Simulation of a model might result in warnings about conflicting Simulink identifiers
Description: When you simulate your model, if the Simulink library is not yet fully loaded, Simulink can report a warning about conflicting internal identifiers . The warning is similar to the following: Warning: Incorrect internal Simulink identifiers were detected. The following blocks have conflicting identifiers: 'simulink/Model-Wide Utilities/DocBlock' 'simulink/Lookup Tables'. The Simulink identifier of 'simulink/Model-Wide Utilities/DocBlock' was changed from '133' to '243'. Backup the original model, and then save and reopen the current model. If the problem persists, contact technical support. ?
Workaround: Open the Simulink Library Browser before using your model. You can also execute the following MATLAB script to turn off the warning:warning('off','Simulink:ID:DuplicateSID')

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The To Video Display Block may produce incorrect results for variable-size signals.
Description: The To Video Display  Block may produce incorrect results for variable-size signals. This occurs for some frame dimensions in code generation and in accelerator mode.
Workaround: To fix this problem, follow these steps:1. Quit MATLAB?.2. Unzip the attached ToVideoDisplay.zip file, which include the following files:- svipwvo2.mexw32_R2012a- svipwvo2.mexw64_R2012a- svipwvo2.mexw32_R2012b- svipwvo2.mexw64_R2012b3. For R2012a, remove the trailing release characters, "_R2012a" and replace the old file with the new one of the same name in the following location:- $MATLABROOT/toolbox/vision/visionutilities/visionmex/, where MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine.4. For R2012b, remove the trailing release characters, "_R2012b" and replace the old file with the new one of the same name in the following location:- $MATLABROOT/toolbox/vision/visionutilities/visionmex/, where MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine.5.. Restart MATLAB?.6. Execute the "rehash toolbox" command

Summary: Loading an R2012b model containing a configurable subsystem can crash Simulink
Description: Saving a model that meets both of the following conditions may crash Simulink: You saved the model in R2012b. The model contains a configurable subsystem, which was open when you saved the model.  ?
Workaround: If you have a model that meets the conditions listed in the bug description, try the following steps. In this example, myModel is the model that does not open successfully.Use cd to navigate to the folder that contains myModel.From the MATLAB command line, issue the following commands:load_system('myModel')set_param('myModel','Lock','off') set_param('myModel','Open','on') save_system('myModel')If you later encounter problems, repeat the workaround procedure.

Summary: Regular data grids that include values at a pole may cause an invalidLatProps error
Description: Data sets with postings at the poles are sometimes represented as regular data grids, with referencing vectors, whose northernmost or southernmost rows extend half a sample-spacing north of the north pole or south of the south pole. Such data sets are rare, but do include global grids returned by the egm96geoid  function. Data sets like these cause errors in various Mapping Toolbox functions, including limitm , setpostn , setltln , ltln2val , and refvecToGeoRasterReference .
Workaround: If you have a regular data grid with values at the poles and can determine its limits without using limitm or refvecToGeoRasterReference, then you can construct a geographic raster reference object directly, using georasterref and the name-value pair 'RasterInterpretation', 'postings'. You can then pass the object to functions such as ltln2val.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Vector permutation of Selector block input signal
Description: A Selector block that specifies a permutation of its input signal might generate incorrect code.  For example, if an input signal A  is an 8-element vector and the Selector block is configured to produce A([5:8 1:4]) , the generated code might omit the permutation. For the error to occur, the total number of elements selected by the Selector block must equal the number of elements in the original signal.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block after the Selector block. Set the Signal Coversion block parameter Output to Signal copy.

Summary: Interpolation might return incorrect results for scattered query points passed as matrices
Description: interp2 , interp3 , interpn , and griddedInterpolant  might interpret scattered query points as a grid if they are passed as matrices. As a consequence, the results are incorrect. For example, [X,Y] = meshgrid(1:2,1:3);V = X.^2 + Y.^2;Xq = [1 2; 1 2];Yq = [1 2; 3 2];Vq = interp2(X,Y,V,Xq,Yq) returns Vq =   2  5 10 13 Xq  is passed as a matrix in meshgrid  format, and Yq  is incorrectly interpreted as having the same format. As a consequence, the second column of Yq  is ignored. The answer should be: Vq =   2 8 10 8
Workaround: Pass the scattered query points as column vectors. Use colon (:) to reshape Xq and Yq.Vq = interp2(X,Y,V,Xq(:),Yq(:));Then use the reshape function to reshape Vq back into a matrix.reshape(Vq,size(Xq))The result has the correct values in matrix form. Vq =   2 8 10 8

Summary: Deploytool project for Excel Add-in target fails to build when it includes more than 128 MATLAB files
Description: If you create a Deploytool project for Excel Add-in target, and add more than 128 MATLAB files in the class, when you build the project, it will fail with the following error message: fatal error C1061: compiler limit : blocks nested too deeply
Workaround: Don't include more than 128 MATLAB files in a class in a Deploytool project. If you want to deploy more than 280 MATLAB files, you can split them into two or more Deploytool projects.

Summary: Deploytool project for Excel Add-in target fails to build when a MATLAB function's help comment is longer than 8000 characters
Description: In a Deploytool project for Excel Add-in target, if you add a MATLAB file with a comment longer than 8000 characters in the class, it will fail to build with the following error message:  string too big, trailing characters truncated
Workaround: Shorten the MATLAB function's help comment to less than 8000 characters, and rebuild the Deploytool project.

Summary: Simulink Code Inspector might not verify code generated for Product or Divide blocks
Description: If the following conditions apply for your model, Simulink Code Inspector might not verify the generated code: A Divide block, or a Product block configured to divide, operates on data with type int32 . The signal entering the second input port of the Divide block, or Product block configured to divide, has a constant sample time and a value of 2.  Note: the second input port supplies the value of the denominator in the division calculation. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Optimization pane, Block reduction  is selected.
Workaround: There are two workarounds:Change the sample time of the block supplying the value of the denominator from inf to a finite sample time. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Optimization pane, clear Block reduction.

Description: When using some older Teledyne DALSA frame grabbers (those designed prior to the prevalence of 64-bit Windows), acquisition may fail with an error similar to the following: Error using imaqdevice/preview (line 177)dalsa: Unable to create SapTransfer object. Summary: Simulink might change the model view when you add a signal to the Scope Signal Viewer.
Description: When you add a signal to to the Scope Signal Viewer, Simulink displays the topmost level of the model, even if you perform the action within a subsystem.
Workaround: To work around this issue, use the Back button in the Simulink Model Editor to return to the previous model view.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When executing multiple solvers concurrently on a multicore target, the solver might exceed expected error tolerances
Description: When executing multiple solvers concurrently on a multicore target, the solver might exceed expected error tolerances and produce unexpected results in generated code. This problem occurs only if all of the following conditions are true: Your model is configured for concurrent execution (Configuration Parameters  > Solver  > Allow tasks to execute concurrently on target  check box is selected). Your model is configured for a fixed step solver (Configuration Parameters  > Solver  > Type  is set to Fixed-step ). Your model contains at least three user defined tasks. Your model contains at least three Model blocks that contain continuous blocks (such as Integrator) and at least one additional Model block that contains only discrete blocks.  ?
Workaround: As a workaround,In the Concurrent Execution dialog, create one task for each Model block.In the Tasks and Mapping node, map each task to its associated Model block for a 1-to-1 mapping.Note that providing one task for each Model block might increase communication overhead and change the efficiency of the model execution, requiring you to make further changes to the model.

Summary: C functions for direct port I/O are missing from the target library on the 64-bit version of Windows.
Description: Compiled models containing user-written S-functions that access I/O-mapped data acquisition devices fail to load in external mode. The error message reports that one or more of the functions _inp, _inpw, _inpd, _outp, _outpw, _outpd is an unresolved symbol. This problem exists only on the 64-bit version of Windows.

Summary: Creating a videoinput object on Linux or Mac OS X may cause a crash with some older Unibrain cameras that are IIDC DCAM v1.04 compliant.

Summary: The menu function always displays command line GUI on Mac OS 10.8 (Mountain Lion)
Description: The menu  function has two versions that it displays. The first is a MATLAB? figure-based GUI with selectable options. The second is a command line equivalent that MATLAB uses when no display is available. In MATLAB 8.0 (R2012b) running on Mac OS 10.8 (Mountain Lion), the menu  function always displays the command line version.
Workaround: To workaround this behavior in MATLAB 8.0 (R2012b):  Do one of the following:Edit menu.m,  located in <MATLABROOT>/toolbox/matlab/uitools folder.Type "edit menu.m" at the MATLAB command prompt. Comment out the following lines (line numbers 44-46), which forces menu to always create a GUI:% if isunix,     % Unix? %    useGUI = length(getenv('DISPLAY')) > 0; % end % if

Summary: If the same signal is used in both the top and referenced models, and the signal uses SimulinkGlobal storage classes, inspection can result in a Failed to verify status??
Description: If a top model uses a signal with SimulinkGlobal  storage class as input to a referenced model, code inspection can result in a Failed to verify  status.  Similarly, if the output of referenced model is a signal with SimulinkGlobal  storage class, code inspection can result in a Failed to verify  status. In both cases, Simulink Code Inspector is unable to verify the initialization code.
Workaround: If a signal in your model uses a SimulinkGlobal storage class, consider reconfiguring your model so that the same signal is not used in the top and referenced model.?

Summary: Export function error message for non-virtual bus is unclear.
Description: An error can occur when exporting code for multiple function-call subsystems in a virtual subsystem. An error appears during export when a non-virtual bus from a function-call subsystem drives a model block in another function-call subsystem. When you right-click the subsystem and select C/C++ Code -> Export Functions this message appears ------------------------- Sample time mismatch. When a Bus Creator or Bus Assignment block outputs a nonvirtual bus, all of the signals driving its input ports must have the same sample time. This restriction applies even if the elements of the object defining the bus specify an inherited (-1) sample time. The sample time of the signal (ASYNC: [Subsystem1/__FcnCallGen__0]) driving input port 1 of 'simpleTest/Subsystem/Function-Call Subsystem1/Subsystem' does not match the sample time of the block (ASYNC: [Subsystem1/__FcnCallGen__0]). -------------------------  This error message refers only to the blocks and sample times that are not visible in the original model. This error occurs because the Export function does not support non-virtual buses across model boundaries.
Workaround: Avoiding using virtual bus to cross exported function boundary.

Summary: Simulink Project fails to detect Subversion (SVN) 1.7 if the project is not in the Subversion root folder
Description: If you create a new Simulink Project in a folder that is not the same as the Subversion root folder, the built-in integration with Subversion? (SVN) for Simulink Project fails to detect that the project is stored in Subversion version 1.7.The project's SVN integration uses the existence of the .svn  folder to determine if the project is stored in Subversion. In Subversion 1.7 and later, Subversion only creates the .svn  folder in the root folder. For this reason the project cannot detect Subversion version 1.7 unless you create the new project in the Subversion root folder.If you use New > Simulink Project > From Source Control to check out your project, the project can correctly detect Subversion version 1.7.
Workaround: From MATLAB, select New > Simulink Project > From Source Control to check out the folder containing the Simulink Project. This makes the folder the top level of your sandbox, and the project can correctly detect Subversion version 1.7.

Summary: Analog output can cause dimension mismatch error
Description: An Index exceeds matrix dimensions.  error can occur if: You queue a small amount of data then immediately queue a much larger amount of data. You set the rate very high and queue a very small amount of data.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: For Each subsystem block with states has local variable overwritten in generated code
Description: The code generator might generate invalid code under all of the following conditions:  The model contains a For Each subsystem block. The For Each subsystem contains a block with discrete states, such as the Unit Delay, Memory, or Discrete Integrator block. The block with states is fed by a source block located in a lower level system, for example, an atomic subsystem that is located in the For Each subsystem. The For Each subsystem block has a separate update function.  The code generator creates a local scalar variable that is overwritten at every iteration step of the For Each subsystem output function. This local scalar variable might be used in the update function of this For Each subsystem as an input, but the local scalar variable holds only the result of the last iteration. The code generator should have used a matrix variable that holds the result of all iterations.

Summary: MATLAB might hang during an External mode simulation that uses a serial transport layer for target communication
Description: If you run an External mode simulation and the model is configured to use a serial transport layer for target communication, the MATLAB session might hang. The hang occurs when External mode communication overflows the serial receive buffer of the target.
Workaround: One way to work around this bug is by increasing the serial receive buffer size of the target. For example, for the Arduino Mega 2560 hardware, increase the serial receive buffer size from 64 to 96.

Summary: MATLAB stops responding when session contains multiple analog output channels
Description: When you use a DAQ session that contains analog output channels and call startForeground multiple times MATLAB can stop responding on the second call.
Workaround: Delete and recreate the session between calls to startForeground.

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: The matchFeatures function may generate incorrect C code.
Description: Incorrect code generation occurs when calling the matchFeatures  function using the default value for the 'Method ' name-value pair without explicitly specifying it.
Workaround: Explicitly specify a value for the 'Method' name-value pair.

Summary: Fixed-point code generated from PID Controller contains floating-point data when clamping antiwindup mode is selected
Description: When you try to generate fixed-point code from the Discrete PID Controller block by explicitly setting the data types to fixed-point data types, the generated code still requires floating-point support if the block has the clamping  antiwindup mode selected.

This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. Summary: If generated code does not have zero initialization code for root level I/O or global data structures, code inspection does not fail.
Description: If a model is set to generate zero initialization for root level I/O or global data structures, but the generated code is missing the initialization code, code inspection does not  fail. For example, in the generated code below, zero initializaton for the external input U_In1  is missing. /* Model initialize function */void mAllInit_initialize( int16_T *U_In1, int16_T *Y_Out1){  /* Registration code */  /* external outputs */  (*Y_Out1) = 0;  }    Simulink Code Inspector does not flag this as an inspection failure. ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using function calls with side effects in colon expressions might cause wrong answers
Description: In the following example code, fcn  has side effects. The first call to fcn()  returns 1 , the second call returns 2 . However, MATLAB Coder treats fcn()  as though it has no side effects and y = u(fcn():1:fcn())  becomes y = u(fcn())  instead of y = u(1:1:2) . y = u(fcn():1:fcn())function x = fcnpersistent p;if isempty(p)    p = 0;endp = p + 1;x = p;end
Workaround: Rewrite y = u(fcn():1:fcn()) as t = fcn(); y = u(t:1:fcn());

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code can result when functions modifying global or persistent data are mixed with vector operations.
Description: When a function that modifies global or persistent data is called as part of a vector computation, the function can execute iteratively rather than once, leading to incorrect answers. For example: global g; y = g(u) y = zeros(10); y = u + f(); y2 = f() g = g + 1;
Workaround: Move the function call above the original expression so that the function is called once.t = f();y = u + t;

Summary: Syntax error using LinearModel.stepwise to perform stepwise regression on a dataset array
Description: LinearModel.stepwise  provides a number of ways to specify a model for performing stepwise regression. In one syntax you input a dataset array and list the predictor variables by number, for example: lm = LinearModel.stepwise(d,'quadratic','PredictorVars',2:5,'ResponseVar',1) Using this syntax may produce an error: Error using & Matrix dimensions must agree.Error in classreg.regr.modelutils.model2terms (line 126)
Workaround: Other ways of specifying the model avoid this error. For example, you could list the predictors by name:lm = LinearModel.stepwise(d,'quadratic','PredictorVars',{'x1' 'x2' 'x3' 'x4'},'ResponseVar',1)Alternatively, you could specify a formula in place of the keyword 'quadratic', or you could pass in a subset of the dataset array containing only the response and the desired predictors.

Summary: Generating code for self-modifying mask may cause MATLAB to crash
Description: When you generate code for a self-modifying mask, your MATLAB session may crash. A self-modifying mask contains initialization code that can add, change, or delete the mask's underlying blocks. One example of a self-modifying mask is 'DSP System Toolbox/Estimation/Power Spectrum Estimation/Periodogram'.

Summary: Error when generating shared types with parallel model reference builds
Description: An error might occur under the following conditions: You are generating code for a model reference hierarchy using parallel model reference build. The hierarchy contains a mix of user-defined types that have their DataScope  property set to 'Exported' . The hierarchy contains parameters with Storage class  set to Auto  that are generated to const_params.c  in the shared utilities folder.  The error message is similar to: Error occurred when generating shared type: Could not find file slprj\ert\_sharedutils\const_params.h
Workaround: To avoid this error do not use parallel model reference build.To remove this error message, in the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Model Referencing pane, clear Enable parallel model reference builds. Then build the model into a clean folder.

Summary: Block data type resets to default during model export to previous versions
Description: When you export a model containing an Input Shadow block to versions R2010a or earlier, the data type parameter of the Input Shadow block incorrectly resets to its default value.

Summary: External I/O structure element order may not match model block order
Description: The code generator may create an external I/O structure definition, where the element order differs from the Inport/Outport block order in the model.  The generated code is correct and produces the correct result.  The generated code is more difficult to read.
Workaround: To work around this issue, on the Code Generation > Interface pane, unselect the Combine signal/state structures check box. The modeling order is honored in the generated code, if there is no bit-field external I/O.
