Summary: Code Report icons do not work unless Current Folder browser has focus
Description: When the address bar is customized to make Code Report icons visible, these icons do not work unless the Current Folder browser has the focus.
Workaround: To work around the problem you can either:Access Report icons from the drop down action menu in the Current Folder browser. Once triggered there, the icons will also work on the toolbar.Put the focus in the Current Folder browser to use the Code Report icons.Move the address bar inside the Current Folder window by right clicking in the address bar and selecting "Move Toolbar Inside Current Folder Panel".

Summary: In MATLAB 8.0 (R2012b), mex -setup cannont find Intel Visual Fortran Composer XE 2011 with Windows SDK 7.1 compiler on 32-bit Windows.
Description: The 32-bit mex options file, intelf12mssdk71opts.stp , looks for a 64-bit compiler.

Summary: Incorrect output possible from the ltln2val and setpostn functions, and certain geographic raster referencing methods
Description: When working with certain geographic rasters whose limits are not round numbers, for certain inputs, the intrinsic X output of the longitudeToIntrinsicX  and geographicToIntrinsic  methods of the spatialref.GeoRasterReference  class can be very different from the correct values. This problem can, in turn, significantly affect the results of the setpostn  and ltln2val  functions, even when using reference vectors or matrices (rather than referencing objects) as input. In addition, for points located exactly on the raster edge along which either the columns or rows start, the outputs of the intrinsicXToLongitude  and intrinsicYToLatitude  methods can be off very slightly, as can the corresponding elements of the Latlim  and Lonlim  property values. In all the cases described above, results in R2012b may differ from earlier releases.
Workaround: The updates provided in this workaround are only for release R2012b.  Installation instructions       Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.       Download the attached file, workaround_877789-12b_2012-10-18.zip, to a folder of your choosing.       On Windows double-click the file to view its contents, then click the Extract button, and extract the contents to a folder of your choosing. On Macintosh, double-click the file to extract its contents. On Linux use the unzip command.       Complete the instructions in the enclosed text file, README.txt.

Summary: Stateflow support limitations
Description: Simulink Code Inspector fails to verify the following Stateflow transitions if: Actions contain binary operators with mixed data types. Action assignment operators have mixed data types. Math functions in actions are replaceable Target Function Libraries (TFL). Action unary minus operators have integer data types. Transitions have inconsistent Stateflow parameter and workspace data object dimensions. Transitions use enumerated data types with non-unique enumerations. For example, enumeration aa(0) bb(00) cc(2) . Transitions use global Data Store Memory (DSM) with boolean data type.

Summary: Using RTW.TargetPrefs.load in MATLAB produces an error: Undefined variable "RTW" or function "RTW.TargetPrefs.load".?
Description: Using RTW.TargetPrefs.load  in a MATLAB Command Window produces an error message: Undefined variable "RTW" or function "RTW.TargetPrefs.load".  If you browse to your MATLAB installation under MATLABROOT/toolbox/rtw/rtw/@RTW  you will not find the directory @TargetPrefs .
Workaround: Download the attached file: attachment_877463_12b_13a_13b_14a_2014-06-17.zipExtract the zip file into a writeable folder, such as C:\Users\username\DownloadsIn MATLAB set Current Folder to the location where you extracted the zip file. This folder will have two files: patch_installer.m and patch_877663.zip.At MATLAB command prompt, enter: patch_installer('patch_877463.zip','install');Follow the instructions displayed on the MATLAB command line.

Summary: sync2 property not accessible for Simulink.sdi.DiffSignalResult object returned from signal comparison in Simulation Data Inspector.
Description: When comparing runs generated by the Simulation Data Inspector, the Simulink.sdi.DiffSignalResult  object that is generated by the Simulink.sdi.compareRuns  and Simulink.sdi.getResultByIndex  commands does not contain the sync2  property.

Summary: An EtherCAT configuration file constructed using TwinCAT may not work correctly with xPC Target.
Description: If the TwinCAT configuration tool is used to construct an XML configuration file for an EtherCAT network, xPC Target may not communicate correctly with the network.
Workaround: The problem can be the task ID that TwinCAT assigned to the task you created. TwinCAT can assign a different ID, such as 2, for a single task where the ET 9000 would assign task ID 1. As a workaround, do the following:1. Open TwinCAT on the configuration.2. Click on the node '<taskname>-image' for your task.3. Click the 'General' tab.4. Insert the number in the 'Id' field into the Task ID list box in the xPC Target EtherCAT Update block.If that change does not solve the problem, then use the ET 9000 configurator instead of TwinCAT to construct a configuration file for use with xPC Target.

Summary: Incorrect SVN version 1.6 shown in Simulink Project text, should say 1.7
Description: When you select the Built-In SVN Integration in Simulink Project, the description text claims support for version 1.6 and below, but it should say 1.7 and below.The incorrect description text says:The Built-In SVN Integration supports SVN sandboxes and repositories at version 1.6 and below.The correct text is:The Built-In SVN Integration supports SVN sandboxes and repositories at version 1.7 and below.
Workaround: Ignore the description text that mentions version 1.6.Simulink Project behaves as follows:If you create a new sandbox using Simulink Project Built-In SVN Integration, you will get a sandbox using the 1.7 version of SVN.If you want to use a previous version of SVN you already have installed, create a new project in a folder already under SVN source control and click Detect. For example, you can create the sandbox using Tortoise, before using the Simulink Project Tool to create a new project in that folder, then detect the existing source control. If it is, say, version 1.6, it will remain a version 1.6 sandbox.

Summary: "Assertion failed" error when generating code for a for-loop with negative step and unsigned integer index
Description: If your MATLAB code contains a for -loop with a negative step and an unsigned integer start value, code generation using MATLAB Coder or the MATLAB Function block in Simulink fails with an "assertion failed" error message. For example, code generation fails for: for i = uint32(10):-1:uint32(0)  ?
Workaround: Before the loop, cast the loop start and end values to a signed integer type, for instance:for i = int32(10):-1:int32(0)

Summary: Code generation stops when generating code from an example with indexed assignment into a scalar expression.
Description: If the MATLAB?  source code contains an indexed assignment into a variable or a structure field that is a scalar, code generation can stop with an "assertion failed" error mentioning "eml_infer_expr.cpp". This error occurs if the indexing expression is complicated (e.g., uses colons, function calls, and indexing into other matrices or arrays). For example, consider this MATLAB code: A = 1; D = [2 1]; A(:, D(2:end)) = 2;   The variable A  is a scalar but the assignment into A  is indexed. Depending on the surrounding code and the complexity of the indexing expression, such as example can cause code generation to stop. ?
Workaround: Idenfity the assignment that causes the error. Use the code generation report to verify that the left-hand side of the assignment is a scalar. Then, remove the subscript index. For example, replace A(:,1:n) = 1 with A = 1 if A is a scalar variable.

Summary: Simulation terminates with SimMechanics error
Description: Simulation of models containing SimMechanics blocks can fail when mechanical system inputs become unstable or asymptotically large. This can occur when interfacing mechanical systems to controllers or other physical domains, often with feedback loops. When a simulation error occurs, SimMechanics issues an error message pointing to a degenerate mass or other causes. The simulation error can occur at intermediate solver time steps, in which case it is not apparent from model outputs or logged data. The current update addresses the simulation issue, reducing the likelihood of simulation failure in SimMechanics models.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When a referenced model has a control port at the top level and an output port is set to return by value
Description: The code generator might generate incorrect code for a model under all of the following conditions: A referenced model has a control port (function call, trigger, or enable) at the top level. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation  pane, Target selection Language  is set to C . On the Code Generation > Interface  pane, in the Model Interface dialog box (to open, click Configure Model Function s), Function specification  is set to Model specific C prototype s. In the Model Interface dialog box, in the Step function argumen ts table, a Port Type  Outport is set to Val ue. For example, in the table, Order  is Return and Category  is Value .  Likewise, for C++, the code generator might generate incorrect code for a model under all of the following conditions: A referenced model has a control port (function call, trigger, or enable) at the top level. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation  pane, Target selection Language  is set to C++ (Encapsulated) . On the Code Generation > Interface  pane, in the Model Interface dialog box (to open, click Configure C++ Encapsulation Interface ), Function specification  is set to I/O arguments step method . In the Model Interface dialog box, in the Step function arguments  table, a Port Type  Outport is set to Value . For example, in the table, Order  is Return and Category  is Value .  ?
Workaround: When a model has a control port at the top level, pass the output by pointer.

Summary: The quadprog interior-point-convex algorithm sometimes returns a spurious solution.
Description: Due to a bug in the preprocessing module, on rare occasions the quadprog interior-point-convex  algorithm returns a solution with some elements permuted. Signs that this occurred: Exitflag = 1 Unusually high constraint violation (output.constrviolation ) Unusually high first-order optimality (output.firstorderopt ) Iterative display shows small (lower than tolerance) constraint violation (labeled Feasibility ) and first-order optimality measure
Workaround: To disable the preprocessing module, use this undocumented and unsupported syntax: [...] = quadprog(H,f,A,b,Aeq,beq,lb,ub,x0,options,'PresolveOps',0);

For example, a file that contains a particular value, such as -32767, to indicate the absence of a valid elevation for a particular cell, will cause this error.

Summary: Consecutive if or switch condition checks inside a loop in a MATLAB Function block
Description: The code generator might generate incorrect code when all of the following conditions are met in a MATLAB Function block: Consecutive if  or switch  condition checks inside a loop. All if  or switch  condition expressions are syntactically identical. The condition expression references a variable which is given a value in at least one of the if  or switch  branches.  For an example, see the attached model mIfBug.slx .
Workaround: To turn off the optimization that causes the incorrect code, download the attached MATLAB script WorkaroundForBugReport874793.p.To enable the workaround, execute the script WorkaroundForBugReport874793(true).To disable the workaround, execute the script WorkaroundForBugReport874793(false).

Summary: On the Windows XP platform, selecting a file from an open file dialog in MATLAB changes the system current directory used by MATLAB.
Description: If you open a File Open dialog in MATLAB and pick a file, the system current directory for the MATLAB application changes to the last directory visited in the File Open dialog. This bug does not occur if you cancel the file open dialog, only when you pick a file from the dialog. Note that the "system current directory" is different from the "current directory" shown in MATLAB.  cd  displays MATLAB's current directory - this is unaffected.  !cd  displays the system current directory - this is affected.
Workaround: This issue does not occur on Windows Vista or later.

Summary: MATLAB job scheduler can cause head node CPU to run at 100%
Description: On a cluster running a MATLAB job scheduler (MJS), one of the CPUs on the head node where the MJS is running might remain at 100% usage after a job has been submitted to the cluster. This does not affect the normal running of the MJS; and jobs submitted to the cluster continue to run normally.

Summary: Simulation fails with error: Cannot update block graphics in current context
Description: When a model contains a referenced model, which is configured in SIL mode, simulating the model in normal mode might produce the following error: Cannot update block graphics in current context .
Workaround: For all referenced models configured in SIL mode, on the Code Generation > Report pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, clear the Generate model Web view check box.

Summary: Cannot use Export Model menu options for locked libraries
Description: The Simulink menu item File > Export Model To is disabled for locked libraries. You cannot export a library to a previous version or to the web when the library is locked.
Workaround: To work around this problem, unlock the library by selecting Diagram > Unlock Library. When the library is unlocked, you can use the Export Model menu items.

Summary: Workspace parameters with multiple references can cause compatibility check and code inspection failures
Description: If a model has a workspace parameter that is referenced by both a builtin block and the mask of a masked block, compatibility checks and code inspection can fail. When you run the compatibility checks on the model, at least one check can fail with the message Abnormal exit: Can not resolve: ParameterName . When you inspect code generated from the model, the code inspection report can log the error Internal error in Simulink Code Inspector compatibility checks. Contact Mathworks Technical Support .
Workaround: Change the reference for the mask from the workspace parameter to a copy of the workspace parameter.?

Summary: Simulink Code Inspector verifies generated code that has non-communative operations with reversed operands
Description: If the model specifies a non-commutative operator (e.g. subtraction), and the code generated from the model incorrectly interchanges the order of the corresponding operation's operands, Simulink Code Inspector does not identify the error, and reports that the code is verified. For example, for the attached model: Generate code using Embedded Coder?. Inspect the generated code using Simulink Code Inspector.  The code is verified, as expected. Open CamAngle_Calc.c. Change the generated code for the Sum block from  z = x - 160.0;   to  z = 160.0 - x;  Inspect the altered code.  Simulink Code Inspector mistakenly reports the generated code as verified.

Summary: Connecting to instruments through ICDEVICE may fail on Windows 8
Description: Customers connecting to instruments using ICDEVICE on a Windows 8 system may observe an error due to incorrect VISA path specified in Instrument Control Toolbox.

Summary: The Model Advisor MAAB check Check for nondefault block attributes incorrectly flags minmax and switch blocks
Description: The Model Advisor MAAB check Check for nondefault block attributes  (MAAB Guideline: db_0140) incorrectly flags minmax and switch blocks if your model contains either of the following: Minmax block with a function change from the default min  to non-default max . Switch block with a non-default criteria.  The Check for nondefault block attributes  flags the blocks for not having annotations.  However, the block annotations are automatically updated when the block parameters change. The blocks do not need additional annotations. ?

Summary: Memory leak when using polybool function
Description: When using the polybool  function, a small memory leak occurs in matlab/toolbox/map/map/private/gpcmex . The memory loss can become large if polybool  is used in a loop with many iterations. This can cause MATLAB to display an out-of-memory error.
Workaround: To reclaim the lost memory, issue a clear mex command. For efficiency, if polybool is used in a loop, issue the clear mex command after a fixed number of iterations.

Summary: The phased.gpu.ConstantGammaClutter object can cause MATLAB to hang when used on some exceptionally large problems.
Description: The phased.gpu.ConstantGammaClutter object can cause MATLAB to hang when the step method is invoked in some cases. In particular, if the problem size being processed is comparable to the available memory on the GPU, the object may hang. The problem size scales as the product of the number of antennas in the system and the number of clutter patches.

Summary: MATLAB Builder EX Function Wizard generated macros do not set "Treat Missing Data As NaN" property for MATLAB function inputs.
Description: In MATLAB Builder EX Function Wizard, users can specify, using the Treat Missing Data As  option, how empty cells in Excel worksheets are handled when converted to MATLAB matrices. The Treat Missing Data As  option sets one of two values, depending on how missing data is to be handled: mwReplaceZero  - treat empty cells referenced by input parameters as zeros (0 s). This is the default value. mwReplaceNaN  - treat empty cells referenced by input parameters as NaNs (Not a Number).  The Treat Missing Data As  option does set these properties correctly when executing the MATLAB function in Builder EX Function Wizard, but not in the generated Visual Basic code. ?
Workaround: To enable settings of the mwReplaceZero and mwReplaceNaN properties in your Visual Basic code, add the following lines for each input argument you define.  Set input_argument_name = CreateObject("MWComUtil.MWArg")input_argument_name.Value = Application.Range("SheetName!from_range:to_range") 'Set the flag for how to treat empty cells in Excel, mwRelaceNaN = 1input_argument_name.MWFlags.DataConversionFlags.ReplaceMissing = 1 For example, for InputArg1, to treat empty cells as NaNs (Not a Number), specify the mwReplaceNaN property by coding the following:Set InputArg1 = CreateObject("MWComUtil.MWArg") InputArg1.Value = Application.Range("Sheet1!$A$1:$A$5") 'Set the flag for how to treat empty cells in Excel InputArg1.MWFlags.DataConversionFlags.ReplaceMissing = 1 'mwReplaceNaN

Summary: MWArray Ndoc missing from MATLAB Builder NE R2012b product
Description: The MWArray Ndoc is missing from the MATLAB Builder NE product due to a incomplete Installation team conversion activity. As a result, users of the MATLAB Builder NE product have no MWArray API Ndoc in the R2012b product. The location of the Ndoc specified in the MATLAB Builder NE Release Notes for R2012b is also invalid, as a result of this bug.
Workaround: Users can reference the Ndoc zipped and attached to this EBR as a workaround.You must copy the unzipped contents to a local drive (C:\tmp for example) to view the .chm file (the Ndoc). You cannot access this file from a network-attached drive.The problem has been solved going forward and the Ndoc will appear in the R2013a build.

Summary: Creating a dataset array from a file with duplicate column headers generates an error.
Description: If you try to create a dataset array from a file that has duplicate column headers, the dataset  constructor generates an error. For example, given the file example.txt  that contains Name,Value,Value abc,1,4 def,2,5 xyz,3,6  executing the following MATLAB code: ds = dataset('File','example.txt','Delimiter',',');  generates the following error: Error using setvarnames (line 36) Duplicate variable names.  A similar error occurs with a spreadsheet file.
Workaround: Do not read the column headers from the file. For text files, use the HeaderLines and ReadVarNames parameters to skip the column headers. For a spreadsheet file, use the Range and ReadVarNames parameters to skip the column headers. However, you might want to use the VarNames parameter to assign specific variable names rather than relying on the default names.For example, given the file example.txt that containsName,Value,Value abc,1,4 def,2,5 xyz,3,6 create a dataset array using the following MATLAB code:ds = dataset('File','example.txt','Delimiter',',', ...             'HeaderLines',1,'ReadVarNames',false,'VarNames',{'Name' 'Value1' 'Value2'})

An inlined parameter is a MATLAB structure or a bus type.  Code generation fails with an undeclared identifier error.

Summary: Subsystem conversion to model reference always creates a bus object file
Description: When you convert a subsystem to model reference by either right-clicking the subsystem or by using Simulink.SubSystem.convertToModelReference , Simulink always creates a MATLAB file for bus objects. The file name is in the form <referenced_model>_bus.m . Even if the converted referenced model does not require any bus objects, Simulink creates an unused, empty MATLAB file.
Workaround: If the generated MATLAB file for the bus objects is empty, you can delete that file without interfering with Simulink processing of the model.

Summary: Incorrect sample time propagation and possible MATLAB crash for simulation of models that contain enabled subsystems with constant (Inf) sample times
Description: If a model has the configuration parameter Optimization > Signals and Parameters > Inline Parameters  selected, and an enabled subsystem that meets the following criteria: The enabled subsystem is inside a triggered subsystem. The enabled subsystem enable signal has a constant (Inf ) sample time (for example, the signal is unconnected, or connected to a Ground block or Constant block with a specified constant sample time (Inf )). The enabled subsystem contains no inport blocks.  The following blocks propagate incorrect sample times and model simulation might cause MATLAB to crash: Outport blocks with inherited sample times from the enabled subsystem. In the enabled subsystem, any blocks with inherited sample times that allow constant sample time, but whose source block(s) do not allow constant sample time.
Workaround: As a workaround, make sure that the sample time of the enable signal of such enabled subsystems is not constant (Inf). For example, if the enable port is unconnected:Connect the enable control input port of the Enabled Subsystem block to a Constant block.Set the Constant value parameter of the Constant block to 0.Set the Sample time parameter of the Constant block to -1 (inherited).

Summary: MATLAB crashes when Simulink Design Verifier analysis is invoked for models with floating-point 2D lookup tables with data type mismatches
Description: MATLAB crashes if Simulink Design Verifier analysis is invoked for a model which contains a lookup table block with the following conditions:  Output data type is floating point (single/double)  Table data type is different from output data type
Workaround: This issue can be avoided if the same data type is specified for the output data type and the table data type.

Summary: Code generation assertion in models with virtual buses
Description: Code generation might assert for models with a Bus Selector block or a Bus Assignment block operating on virtual buses:  Code generation assertion 'toType.getNumDimensions() == static_cast<uint32_t>(lastDim+1)' failed in '.\rtwcg_utils\rtwcg_dataconnector.cpp:1017'
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block before the Bus Selector block. Specify the Signal Conversion block parameters:Set Output to Signal copy mode.Select the Exclude this block from the 'Block reduction' optimization checkbox.

Summary: Inherited output types for a Stateflow chart can sometimes produce assertions instead of throwing a proper error message
Description: Stateflow charts with MATLAB action language allow the output data to have inherited type/size/complexity specified (this is the default for newly created outputs). This size/type/complexity is inferred from the usage of that output variable in the chart. If such outputs are connected to another block that is not compatible with the inferred size/type/complexity of the output, an assertion can sometimes be thrown with the symptom:  typeReplaced.getMatrixSize() == typeReplacing.getMatrixSize()
Workaround: Two possible workarounds:Specify the size/type/complexity of the output data completely in the Model Explorer.Connect the output data to a block that is compatible with the size/type/complexity of the data.

Summary: sawtooth function gives incorrect output when WIDTH input is set to 0
Description: sawtooth(T,0) returns a sawtooth signal with a value of zero at T = n*2*pi, n= 0,1,2,...The correct behavior is to return a signal with a value of 1 (maximum) at T = n*2*pi, n= 0,1,2,...

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB Coder might generate incorrect code for array elements used in a loop
Description: MATLAB Coder might generate incorrect code if the MATLAB source file:  Accesses array elements in a loop Reads the array element before writing to it in each iteration Accesses the array element more than once using the same index in each iteration  In the attached example, the source code meets all of these conditions in accessing array element x(2) , and the generated code gives the wrong answer.

Summary: Code inspection fails for models with the < or > characters in subsystem names
Description: If your model meets both these conditions, code inspection might fail with the error: Error inserting BLOCKNAME in Results.blockTable.Data.  Synthesizes blocks during compilation Uses the <  or >  characters in subsystem names  BLOCKNAME  is the name of the synthesized block. During compilation, blocks might be synthesized in either of these scenerios: If the incoming signal to a table lookup or model block is discontiguous, Simulink inserts a synthesized Signal Conversion block at the inports.  If a model block references a model with a function call port at the root level, Simulink inserts a synthesized Subystem block.
Workaround: There are 2 workarounds:Remove the < or > characters from subsystem names. Install a patch according to the following instructions. This patch is only applicable for R2012b release.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Code inspection fails a for referenced model with a function call port that has a periodic sample time
Description: Code inspection fails for a referenced model with a function call port that has a periodic sample time. The error might be similar to: Error: Error due to multiple causes.Error: The caller 'top_0/Model' of function-call subsystem 'top_0/Model' has a sample time of [inf, 0] that does not match the periodic sample time 1 of the function-call subsystem. This error message is related to a hidden Ground block.Consider manually inserting such a block to debug the problem.Error: Cannot complete engine interface method

Summary: Access Violation on Windows and a Segment Violation on Linux or OS X when executing Report Generator functionality in more than one deployment component
Description: When executing report generator functionality in more than one deployment component, an Access Violation may occur on Windows or a Segment Violation may occur or Linux or OS X. This can occur in two shared library components, two MATLAB Builder NE components, or two MATLAB Builder JA components.
Workaround: Restrict deployment report generation use to one active component at a time.

Description: The software removes all temporary files at the end of a verification. However, as a verification proceeds through the various phases, the total number of temporary files increases after each phase. For a large verification, the temporary files might fill the temporary folder before the end of the verification. In this case, you see the error message: No space left on device
Workaround: Use the attachment to update your software.Installation InstructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your Polyspace installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your Polyspace installation. By default this is in c:\Polyspace or in /usr/local/Polyspace on Linux systems. This folder is referred to as <POLYSPACE> in subsequent steps. Polyspace_Common and PolyspaceForCandCPP_R2012a are the two principal sub-folders of <POLYSPACE>.Stop Polyspace queue manager service on each CPU.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <POLYSPACE> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell:unzip -l <POLYSPACE>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <POLYSPACE> folder.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell: cd <POLYSPACE>unzip <.zip file>Check that binaries have appropriate ˇ®read & executeˇŻ permissions.Delete the .zip file in the <POLYSPACE> folder.Restart each Polyspace queue manager service.

Summary: Byte Pack block does not propagate output data size correctly
Description: Byte Pack block may not propagate the correct dimensions of the output port if connected to an Input Port with data type set to inherited.
Workaround: Explicitly specify the data type of the Input Port.

Summary: If your model loads input from the base workspace, code inspection might fail for a model reference target
Description: If your model loads input from the base workspace, code inspection might fail for a model reference target. The error might be similar to:  Error: Error due to multiple causes.Error: External input specified in the Configuration Parameters dialog box on the Data Import/Export page (or as an option to SIM command) to a model containing no root level input portsError: Cannot complete engine interface method
Workaround: When inspecting code for the model, temporarily disable loading external inputs from the base workspace. To do this, deselect Input on the Data Import/Export > Load from workspace pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: Simulink Code Inspector compatibility checker fails to detect when CombineOutputUpdateFcns is set to off
Description: Simulink Code Inspector compatibility check Check code generation settings  fails to detect when configuration parameter Single output/update function  is set to off . To be compatible with code inspection, Single output/update function  must be set to on.  In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, Single output/update function  is on the Code Generation > Interface  pane. The command-line form of the parameter is CombineOutputUpdateFcns.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parmaters dialog box, on the Code Generation > Interface pane, set Single output/update function to on.

Summary: MATLAB Builder NE applications crash using multiple MCRs
Description: Users can encounter this issue if their application uses the same MATLAB class in different MCR instances. This problem can be seen when the following occurs: In the first MCR instance, a class instance (bar , for example) is constructed and a method (foo , for example) called on the class. In another MCR instance, bar  is constructed and a method other than  foo  is called. In the first MCR instance, foo  is again called.  A crash occurs because MATLAB invalidates foo in step 2. When the application calls foo again in step 3, using the first MCR instance, MATLAB fails to recognize the invalidated method and tries to invoke it.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code output from model with branched function-call signal invoked by a Stateflow chart in Accelerator mode, or a noninlined S-function
Description: When a function-call signal invoked by a Stateflow chart is branched, the model generates incorrect output results when all  the following conditions are true: The model has simulation mode set to Accelerator . The Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Block reduction  option is selected. The first output port of the Function-Call Split block connects to a function-call block reduced by block reduction (caused, for example, by an empty function-call subsystem or a function-call subsystem with no output ports), and the second output port of the Function-Call Split block connects to a function-call block that is not reduced.  When a function-call signal invoked by a noninlined S-function is branched, the model generates incorrect output results when all  the following conditions are true: The Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Block reduction  option is selected. The first output port of the Function-Call Split block connects to a function-call block reduced by block reduction (caused, for example, by an empty function-call subsystem or a function-call subsystem with no output ports), and the second output port of the Function-Call Split block connects to a function-call block that is not reduced.
Workaround: As a workaround, either don't select Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Block reduction option, or connect the function-call block, driven by the first output port of the Function-Call Split block, so that it is not reduced as part of the block reduction optimization. For example, connecting at least one output of the function-call block to a Sink block, such as Scope or To Workspace, keeps the function-call block from block optimization.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code replacement of addition operation ignores Sum block accumulator data type
Description: During code generation for a Sum block, the build process analyzes the addition operation to determine if there is a matching replacement function in the code replacement library (target function library) selected for the model. However, the code replacement analysis fails to consider the accumulator data type configured for the Sum block. As a result, the build process might select a replacement function that implements a different accumulator data type than what is configured for the Sum block. This discrepancy might cause the numerical result of executing the generated code to differ from the simulation result.
Workaround: To work around this bug, check that the Accumulator data type configured in the Sum block parameters matches the accumulator data type implemented in your replacement function.

Summary: Incorrect sample time propagation for asynchronous function-call signal selected from wide function call
Description: When the following is true for an asynchronous function-call signal: You use the Bus Selector or Selector block to select the signal from a wide asynchronous function call signal The signal is the only one selected (such that the Bus Selector or Selector has only one output port)  Simulink incorrectly propagates the union of the sample times of the wide signal to the function call subsystem. Instead, Simulink should propagate the sample time of the single asynchronous function call that was selected. If your model is affected by this issue, you might get a warning like the following: Warning: The block (see list directly below) is invoked by asynchronous S-functions specifying conflicting priorities:  'myModel/taskInitiator1' specifies 12  'myModel/taskInitiator1' specifies 13
Workaround: As a work around:Select a second signal in the Bus Selector or Selector block.A second block output port appears.Connect the second block output port to a Terminator block.

Summary: Hyperlinks embedded in error messages for State Transition Tables may not highlight appropriate table cell.
Description: If you click on the hyperlinks embedded in error messages that originate from the State Transition Tables block in Stateflow, and the corresponding table cell on the Stateflow UI is not visible, the table cell may not be selected. Workaround: Expand all parent rows in the State Transition Table UI before attempting to select the hyperlinks embedded in corresponding error messages.

Summary: When using Rapid Accelerator mode to simulate models using model reference, Simulink does not call the InitFcn callback of such referenced models.
Description: For models using model reference, when simulating in Normal mode or Accelerator mode, Simulink executes the InitFcn callback of referenced models when simulation starts. However, when using Rapid Accelerator mode to simulate such models, Simulink does not execute the InitFcn callback of referenced models.
Workaround: To solve this, move the initialization code into the InitFcn callback of the top model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Subsystem blocks with the Minimize algebraic loops occurrences check box selected
Description: The generated code might read uninitialized local variables for the following case: The model contains an atomic Subsystem block. In the Subsystem Block Parameter dialog box, the Minimize algebraic loops occurrences  check box is selected. This Subsystem block contains an atomic subsystem, where the Function packaging  parameter is specified as Function  or Reusable function . The non-inlined atomic subsystem contains a block with discrete states that updates its' states in the update function.  In this case, the states might be written with the value of an uninitialized local variable.
Workaround: Avoid using non-inlined atomic subsystem blocks inside of atomic subsystems with the Minimize algebraic loops occurrences check box selected.

Summary: Error exception Match(Match) raised during Software Safety Analysis phase 0
Description: In very rare cases, a Polyspace verification may stop with the following error message: ...**** Software Safety Analysis Level 0 - 18 (P_PP) * XXXX pp, Y ppp. **** Software Safety Analysis Level 0 - 18 (P_PP) took Z.Yreal, Z.Yu + Xs (Z.Ygc) **** Software Safety Analysis Level 0 - 19 (P_RPTR) exception Match(Match) raised. ...  This type of error is specific to the code being verified, so it is not possible to provide an example that reproduces the error. If you see this error, contact Technical Support. If possible, provide the source code and the verification log.

Summary: egm96geoid function returns truncated data grid
Description: If you supply a longitude limit vector and the limits span a full 360 degrees, the egm96geoid  function returns a truncated data grid. For example, given the limits [0 360] , the resultant data grid consists of only a single column. Given the limits [-180 180] , the resultant grid is missing a column.
Workaround: Rather than using the egm96geoid function to sub-sample the data, read the complete data grid using the syntax egm96geoid(1), since it should easily fit into memory.  Construct a geographic raster reference object directly, using georasterref and the name-value pair 'RasterInterpretation', 'postings'. You can then pass the data grid and object to functions such as ltln2val.Users of egm96geoid are encouraged to review Bug Report 887272 for issues with other toolbox functions and data grids that include values at a pole.

Summary: Performance improvements for Tiff class
Description: The Tiff  class can be slow when used with multipage TIFF files or individual TIFF files with many strips or tiles.

Summary: Interactively resizing some blocks fails
Description: When you attempt to resize some blocks interactively, all of the following problems happen: The resize cursor vanishes. The resize fails to happen. The notification bar displays the following warning:  Interactive resize was cancelled because block icon (possibly mask) drawing triggered a change in the model In most instances, this means that the process of drawing the block's mask unexpectedly changed the underlying model.
Workaround: The known pattern that triggers this behavior is mask drawing code that calls set_param during the drawing process. To identify where the block might be doing this,  from the command window set a breakpoint at calls to set_param:dbstop in set_paramThen attempt to resize the block again. The MATLAB debugger should point you to the offending set_param. In most cases, such set_param calls are not actually changing anything at draw time and can be safely avoided. For example, a set_param changing a string parameter:set_param(block,'MaskDisplay',newString); can be guarded to prevent sets that don't actually change the parameter:if ( strcmp(get_param(block,'MaskDisplay'),newString) ~= 1 ) ???set_param(block,'MaskDisplay',newString); end If you do not have access to the source of the block in question, or there is no way to avoid making the changes at draw time, you can still resize the block can still be resized using set_param(block,'Location',...) or via Simulink Editor Diagram > Arrange options.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: comm.RSDecoder does not support codegen when BitInput property is set to true.
Description: When using codegen command to compile a matlab function containing comm.RSDecoder System object, with BitInput property set to true, will throw the following error: "The nontunable property 'cIBC' of class 'comm.RSDecoder' cannot be defined by subscripted assignment." Similar issue happens to comm.RSEncoder and similar workaround can be applied.
Workaround: Codegen works fine when BitInput property is set to false. Here is the workaround step: 1. set BitInput property to false 2. Add a bit-to-integer conversion prior to decoding 3. Add an integer-to-bit conversion after decoding This will allow the codegen to work and obtain the same result.

Summary: TI C2000 CAN Calibration Protocol (CCP) does not support vectors of size greater than 5 bytes.
Description: Code generation for TI C2000 processors using CAN Calibration Protocol (CCP) block does not support vectors of size greater than 5 bytes; the data may be lost or shuffled during CAN transfers from the target to the CCP Calibration Tool.
Workaround: To work around this issue follow these steps:1. Close Matlab.2. Download the attached .zip file.3. Make a backup of the following files and replace them with the ones found in the attachement:For Matlab versions from R2007b to R2009aMATLABROOT\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic2000\tic2000\src\ccp_utils.cMATLABROOT\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic2000\tic2000\include\ccp_utils.hFor Matlab versions from R2009b to R2012b:MATLABROOT\toolbox\target\extensions\processor\tic2000\src\ccp_utils.cMATLABROOT\toolbox\target\extensions\processor\tic2000\include\ccp_utils.h(Where MATLABROOT is the Matlab installation directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the Matlab command prompt)4. You can now start using Matlab normallyAs best practice with the 16-bit addressable nature of TI C2000 processors (word addressable MCU), make sure that your calibration tool is configured to work with word addressable processors.

Summary: ARMA model estimation errors when using the armax command
Description: Using an existing ARMA model to configure the initial parameterization in the armax  command produces the following error: Error using armax (line 90)  An IDPOLY model of "ARMAX" structure expected for the "armax" command. Type "help armax" for more information.   For example, estimate an ARMA model of order na = 2, nc = 1 for time series data. data = rand(100,1); % time series data model1 = armax(data, [2 1]);  Using model1  to configure the initial parameterization errors. model2 = armax(data, model1);
Workaround: Use pem instead of armax to configure the initial parameterization.For example:data = rand(100,1); model1 = armax(data, [2 1]);model2 = pem(data, model1);

Summary: toolboxDependencyAnalysis errors if supplied the wrong file types
Description: If you try to use dependencies.toolboxDependencyAnalysis to analyze files other than .m, .mdl or .slx, you see an error. The example in the documentation does not clarify this. The example for the function dependencies.toolboxDependencyAnalysis suggests running:>>files=dependencies.filedependencyanalysis(model_name);>>toolboxes=dependencies.toolboxdependencyanalysis(files); The example works only if the model "model_name" has only dependencies on .m or model files. If the model "model_name" has file dependencies other than .m, .mdl or .slx files, then you supply this list of files to toolboxdependencyanalysis, then you see an error.
Workaround: To avoid this error, remove any files that are not  .m, .mdl or .slx files before passing them to the function dependencies.toolboxDependencyAnalysis. Example code is shown below:% Analyze the model "model_name" for file dependencies:files = dependencies.filedependencyanalysis(model_name);% Create a list of just .m, .mdl and .slx files for toolbox dependency% analysis:files_to_analysis_for_toolbox_deps = {};valid_extensions = {'.m','.mdl','.slx'};for jj=1:numel(files)    [~, ~, this_ext] = fileparts(files{jj});    if ismember(this_ext, valid_extensions)        files_to_analysis_for_toolbox_deps = ...            [files_to_analysis_for_toolbox_deps; files{jj}];     endend% Perform the toolbox dependency analysis:toolboxes = dependencies.toolboxdependencyanalysis(files_to_analysis_for_toolbox_deps);

Summary: MATLAB crashes during code generation of Simulink model containing Trigonometric Function block that uses acosh or atanh function and has complex input or output.
Description: If you generate code (select C/C++ Code > Build Model  or press Ctrl+B ) for a model that contains a Trigonometric Function block, a MATLAB crash occurs if ALL of the following conditions are true. In the Trigonometric Function block, the function is set to acosh  or atanh . Input or output of the Trigonometric Function block is complex. In the Solver | Zero-crossing options  pane of the Model Configuration Parameters dialog box, Zero-crossing control  is set to Enable All .
Workaround: To work around this issue:In the Solver | Zero-crossing options pane of the Model Configuration Parameters dialog box, set Zero-crossing control to Disable All.

Summary: Custom code directories for Normal mode referenced models that specify relative paths may not be found
Description: During model update, you may encounter an error if you have a referenced model that meets both  these conditions: Is referenced multiple times in Normal mode Specifies relative paths to custom code directories  The typical error that occurs is that files in custom code directories cannot be found.
Workaround: Use one of these workarounds:Set at most one instance of each model to be in Normal mode.Specify directories as absolute, rather than relative, paths.

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: TLM Generator generates TLM component with erroneous output values for multirate subsystem
Description: The TLM Generator generates a TLM component with erroneous output values for a multirate subsystem. This incorrect output results in a data comparison mismatch during the verification step.
Workaround: In the Simulink model window:- Select Simulation > Model Configuration Parameters- Select the Solver pane- For the option Tasking mode for periodic sample times, select "SingleTasking" - Click OK

Summary: The Digital Input block and the Digital Output block for TI F2806x processors produces an error during "Update Diagram" when a GPIO PIN value higher or equal to GPIO50 is selected.
Description: When using the Digital Input block or the Digital Output block for TI F2806x processors with a GPIO PIN value that is higher than GPIO50, the following error message appears. Error Message : ----------------Error evaluating 'InitFcn' callback of C2806x GPIO Digital Output block (mask) 'my_model/Digital Output'. Caused by: Undefined function or variable "increment_idx".
Workaround: Close Matlab.Download the attached zip file.Backup the file ''validateC280xGPIO.m" in the folder "MATLABROOT/toolbox/target/extensions/processor/tic2000/blks/masks/".Unzip and copy the new file ''validateC280xGPIO.m" to "MATLABROOT/toolbox/target/extensions/processor/tic2000/blks/masks/".Open and start using Matlab.

Summary: MATLAB Function blocks with large parameters simulate slowly.
Description: R2012a introduced a regression in the performance of MATLAB Function blocks with parameters that use large amounts of memory. The regression causes these blocks to simulate more slowly in Simulink Normal mode and Accelerator mode than in previous releases. There is no impact on standalone code generation or Rapid Accelerator mode.

Summary: Code generation produces uncompilable C code if the source MATLAB code uses an enumeration inside a package
Description: Code generation from a MATLAB function (either using MATLAB Coder or for a MATLAB Function Block in Simulink) might produce uncompilable C code if the function uses a MATLAB enumeration that resides in a package. The generated C code is uncompilable because it contains a type name with the dot symbol (".") in it.
Workaround: Move the enumeration file from the package to the parent folder.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Two n-D Lookup Table blocks or two Interpolation Using Prelookup blocks might incorrectly share the same interpolation function.
Description: You see this issue when ALL of the following conditions are true: You have a model that contains two n-D Lookup Table blocks or two Interpolation Using Prelookup blocks. For each block, Table data type is fixed-point. Table data of each block has same sign, word length, and fraction length. However, for each block, Table data has different slope or bias. For each block, Fraction data type or Intermediate results data type is floating-point. You have configured both n-D Lookup Table blocks or both Interpolation Using Prelookup blocks with the same:  Number of table dimensions Index data type (from input signal) Fraction data type Intermediate results data type Extrapolation method and settings for any conditionally-displayed options Index search method (n-D Lookup Table block only) Setting for "Valid index may reach last" option Integer rounding mode Setting for "Saturate on integer overflow" option  When all of the above conditions are true, in code generation mode the two blocks generate only one utility function. The slope or bias from only one of the blocks is used.
Workaround: To work around this issue:For each block, if the Intermediate results data type is floating-point, change it to be the same as the Table data type.If the Fraction data type is floating-point, change it to:'ufix16_en15' to prioritize speed.'ufix32_en31' to prioritize precision.

Summary: Analysis stops before maximum analysis time has elapsed, reports undecided objectives for models containing arrays of enumerated data
Description: The Simulink Design Verifier log window shows that analysis completed normally, but the analysis stops before the maximum analysis time has elapsed and still reports undecided objectives. This occurs for models containing arrays of enumerated data.

Summary: Tuning PID Controller block within Stateflow Simulink function causes error
Description: Clicking Tune  in a PID Controller block inside a Stateflow Simulink function generates the following error:  No appropriate method, property, or field Parent for class Stateflow.Chart.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when linearizing a model with a Prelookup block inside a Model block.
Description: A MATLAB crash occurs when linearizing a Simulink model that contains a Model block with a Prelookup block inside. The Breakpoint data  option of the Prelookup block is set to Input port .
Workaround: To work around this issue, do one of the following:Enclose the Model block in a subsystem. To do this, right-click the Model block and select Subsystem & Model Reference > Create Subsystem from Selection.Change the Breakpoint data option of the Prelookup block to Dialog.

Summary: Syntax error in Visual Basic functions generated by MATLAB Builder EX for MATLAB arguments containing a tilde (~) operator.
Description: MATLAB's tilde (~ ) notation is used to denote missing inputs in function declarations, as well as missing outputs in calls to functions.  If you define a MATLAB function with a tilde operator and compile the function into a Microsoft Excel Addin using the Deployment Tool (or mcc ),  the tilde is converted to a variable named _U0  (underscore U zero) in the Visual Basic function.  In VBA, variable names cannot begin with underscore characters. Therefore, a syntax error is generated when the function is run.
Workaround: To workaround this problem, remove the underscore (_) from the variable name in the Visual Basic code.

Summary: Polyspace TOR test for OR-300 and rule MISRA-C 4.1 fails
Description: When running the TOR test <IECKit>\iec\R2012b\tests\qualkitdo_polyspace_tcpr.bat (under Linux <IECKit>/iec/R2012b/tests/qualkitdo_polyspace_tcpr.sh), the test fails with the following messages: ============================================================================ OR-300: -strict=======tor-checks/OR-300-permissive.c --> PASSEDtor-checks/OR-300-strict.c --> FAILEDFollowing reference is not found in results : function "stub_not_allowed_in_strict_mode" must be stubbed manuallySee also Execution-Folder/tor-checks/OR-300-strict/verification.log  OR-300: -permissive (command line only) =======tor-checks/OR-300-permissive.c --> PASSEDtor-checks/OR-300-strict.c --> FAILEDFollowing reference is not found in results : function "stub_not_allowed_in_strict_mode" must be stubbed manuallySee also Execution-Folder/tor-checks/OR-300-strict/verification.log  ...  4.1: fully Only those escape sequences that are defined in the ISO C standard shall be used.=======misrac/misra-4-1.c --> FAILEDFollowing reference is not found in results : 37 : MISRA-C WARNING : rule 4.1 (required) violatedSee also Execution-Folder/misrac/misra-4-1/verification.log ============================================================================  The errors are due to a slight change in the verification output. The test for both OR-300 and MISRA-C rule 4.1 results in 'FAILED' status because of extra words in the referenced message. ?

Summary: Storing objects in MAT-files can cause a memory leak and prevent the object class from being cleared
Description: After storing an instance of a class, 'MyClass', in a MAT-file, calling clear classes  may result in the warning: Warning: Objects of 'MyClass' class exist.  Cannot clear this class or any of its superclasses.   This warning persists, even if you have cleared all instances of the class in the workspace.  The warning may occur for one MAT-file format, and not for another.
Workaround: Under some circumstances, switching to a different MAT-file format may eliminate the warning.

Summary: Variant preprocessor condition incorrectly evaluated in code
Description: If a model contains a model variant or a variant subsystem, the preprocessor condition corresponding to the variant might be incorrectly evaluated in the generated code. The incorrect evaluation might occur when the variant condition, defined by a parameter object, uses a user-defined, imported custom storage class that specifies a header file that is not an instance specific property.
Workaround: When creating the parameter objects that define the Condition of a Simulink.Variant object, avoid using a custom storage class that specifies header files that are not instance specific properties.

Summary: Simulink Code Inspector Compatibility Checker does not report ++ and -- operators as unsupported
Description: The Simulink Code Inspector Compatibility Checker does not report  ++  and --  as unsupported operators in a Stateflow transition label. If you use these operations, the Simulink Code Inspector fails to verify the generated code.
Workaround: Change the unsupported operation to a supported operation. For example, replace x++ with  x = x + 1.

CAGE parallel optimization uses its own scheduler.In earlier releases the toolbox can detect this problem and inform you that you need to close matlabpool . In R2012a and R2012b the toolbox fails to detect the problem and you see the error above.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, you can either close matlabpool before running the optimization, or install the attached patch.If matlabpool is open, you can see an indicator in the lower-right corner of the MATLAB desktop to show that this desktop session is connected to an open pool. The number indicates how many workers are in the pool. Close the pool by entering: matlabpool close To run optimizations in parallel, see Parallel Computing in Optimization.Alternatively, install the attached patch. Description: Simulink Code Inspector might fail to verify generated code that has a custom storage class with a Const  type qualifier. If a signal in your model uses a Const  custom storage class in a model packaging tool (MPT), you might encounter this issue.

Summary: Model Advisor 'Identify questionable fixed-point operations' checks for Compare to Constant or Zero fail to complete if enumerated types are used
Description: If your model contains a Compare To Constant block or Compare to Zero block with an enumerated data type input, the Model Advisor or Code Generation Advisor 'Identify questionable fixed-point operations' check might throw a false warning. When this bug occurs, the Model Advisor generates a warning similar to the following message: "Unable to perform Model Advisor fixed-point check for 'Compare To Constant', error occurred: 'Unrecognized data type name string 'enumType'."  Once the Model Advisor generates this warning, it aborts the 'Identify questionable fixed-point operations' check for all remaining Compare to Constant and Compare to Zero blocks in the model, regardless of their input type.
Workaround: For Compare To Constant blocks (or Compare to Zero blocks) that are processing enumerated data types, replace those blocks with a combination of Constant and Relational Operator blocks.

Summary: Simulink Find dialog box disappears after performing a search
Description: After you perform a search using the Simulink Find dialog box, (either by clicking the Find  button or the Enter  key), the dialog box loses focus and may get hidden behind other open windows in the desktop.
Workaround: The Find dialog is still open, but you may have to rearrange other open windows in the desktop. To avoid this issue, you can also download a patch for the appropriate release. Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Change log:October 18, 2012: attachment_854973_12b_2012_10_18.zip: fixed Find dialog focus issue in R2012b.October 18, 2012: attachment_854973_11b_12a_2012_10_18.zip: fixed Find dialog focus issue in R2011b and R2012a.

Summary: Error exception Bad_Builtin(Bad_Builtin) during intermediate language translation
Description: If your code contains the function memset()  which uses absolute addresses or pointers to pointers, then a Polyspace verification of the code might generate the following error during the intermediate language translation phase: **** GNU C++ to intermediate language translation - 19 (P_PT)  exception Bad_Builtin(Bad_Builtin) raised.    For example, the following pseudo-code might generate this error. memset((void *)(0x1234), 0, 0x180); ...Struct *data;memset((void*)(*data), 0x00, 0x09 );
Workaround: Rerun your verification with the following option: -cfe-extra-flags -disable-memzero-optim

Summary: Generated code that contains for-loops within a conditional branch statement might fail inspection
Description: Code verification might fail if the generated code has this pattern: There is a conditional statement in the code.  For example, an If-Else  or Switch-Case  statement. In one branch of the conditional statement, a vector signal is indexed through a for-loop . In the step 2 computation, a scalar temporary variable is used. In a different branch of the same conditional statement, the same vector signal is indexed directly without a for-loop .  For example, in this generated code: /* SwitchCase: '<Root>/Switch Case2' incorporates:  * Inport: '<Root>/In14'  */  if (bug853931_U_In14 == 222) {  /* Outputs for IfAction SubSystem: '<Root>/Action Subsystem1' incorporates:  * ActionPort: '<S1>/Action Port'  */  for (i = 0; i < 2; i = i + 1) {  /* Saturate: '<S1>/Saturation3' incorporates:  * Inport: '<Root>/In15'  */  if (bug853931_U_In15[i] >= 111) {  y = 111;  } else if (bug853931_U_In15[i] <= 2) {  y = 2;  } else {  y = bug853931_U_In15[i];  }  /* Outport: '<Root>/Out5' incorporates:  * DataTypeConversion: '<S1>/Data Type Conversion12'  * Inport: '<Root>/In4'  * Product: '<S1>/Divide'  * Saturate: '<S1>/Saturation3'  */  bug853931_Y_Out5[i] = div_s32_floor(bug853931_U_In4[i], y);  }  /* End of Outputs for SubSystem: '<Root>/Action Subsystem1' */  } else {  /* Outputs for IfAction SubSystem: '<Root>/Action Subsystem2' incorporates:  * ActionPort: '<S2>/Action Port'  */  /* Outport: '<Root>/Out5' incorporates:  * Constant: '<Root>/Constant'  * DataTypeConversion: '<S2>/Data Type Conversion'  * Inport: '<Root>/In1'  * SignalConversion: '<S2>/Signal Conversion1'  */  bug853931_Y_Out5[0] = (int32_T)(real32_T)floor(bug853931_U_In1);  bug853931_Y_Out5[1] = (int32_T)(real32_T)floor(1.0F);  /* End of Outputs for SubSystem: '<Root>/Action Subsystem2' */  }  In the first branch, signal bug853931_Y_Out5  is computed using for-loops . In the second branch, the same signal is computed using individual elements. The for-loop  operations in the first branch fail verification.
Workaround: Ensure that operations in both branches of the conditional branch statement do not have for-loops. To do this, change the Loop unrolling threshold settings for the model to a value larger than the dimensions of signal being computed. The Loop unrolling threshold settings are accessed from the Optimization > Signals and Parameters pane of the Model Configuration Parameters.

Summary: Wrong results for some models depending on Solver Configuration and Simulink-PS Converter block settings
Description: Selecting the Use local solver  check box in the Solver Configuration block and setting the Filtering and derivatives  parameter of a Simulink-PS Converter block to Filter input  may result in the output of the Simulink-PS Converter block being a constant, equal to the initial unfiltered input value, rather than being a filtered input. Note that both conditions must be met for the wrong results to occur.
Workaround: Insert any Simulink block with continuous sample time, or non-sampled zero crossings, anywhere in the model. A simple way to do this without introducing additional states or warnings is to insert a Step block feeding a Terminator block.

Non-breaking space characters (\223 and \224) or other non-ASCII characters. Non-breaking space characters might be introduced inadvertently into a file, for example, when copying and pasting comments from Microsoft Word documents into MATLAB functions.

Summary: Incorrect UNR check generated by conditional instructions of if statement
Description: Consider the following pseudo-code: var = 0;for (i=0; i<3; i++) { if (random()) { var = 1; i = 3; } else { /* empty */ } }if ( var == 0) { /* UNR check generated for '{' */ ... } Polyspace displays a UNR check for the branch of the second if  statement, indicating that the code is unreachable. This check is incorrect as the code is not  unreachable, that is, the code might be executed.
Workaround: Installation instructions for R2012a Polyspace serverLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your Polyspace installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your Polyspace installation. By default this is in c:\Polyspace or in /usr/local/Polyspace on Linux systems. This folder is referred to as <POLYSPACE> in subsequent steps. Polyspace_Common and PolyspaceForCandCPP_R2012a are the two principal sub-folders of <POLYSPACE>.Stop Polyspace queue manager service.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <POLYSPACE> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell:unzip -l <POLYSPACE>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <POLYSPACE> folder.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell: cd <POLYSPACE>unzip <.zip file>Check that binaries have appropriate ˇ®read & executeˇŻ permissions.Delete the .zip file in the <POLYSPACE> folder.Restart Polyspace queue manager service.

Summary: Displayed time for Clock block does not update during simulation
Description: For the Clock block, when you enable the Display time  option, the block does not update the displayed time during simulation.

Summary: Pressing Ctrl-C to exit MATLAB with an unsaved Simulink model might crash MATLAB.
Description: When a user exits MATLAB with an unsaved model in Simulink, MATLAB displays a dialog with the option to cancel the exit and continue working with the model or save it. However, this dialog might be hidden behind a window. Since the user cannot see the dialog and does not realize that MATLAB has offered this option, the user might press Ctrl-C to return control to the command window. In that case, MATLAB might crash with a stack trace that ends with an assertion. The message in the assertion is similar to: Assertion at b:\matlab\src\mcr\runtime\shutdown.cpp line 126: Unexpected exception during MATLAB shutdown: InterruptException(ctrl-c) In this case, the handler for Ctrl-C was called in the middle of the shutdown code and asserts that something unexpected happened.
Workaround: If you attempt to quit MATLAB while working on a Simulink model, and MATLAB does not exit, check to see if a cancel dialog box is hidden beneath a window. Use that dialog to confirm or cancel the quit.

Summary: Loading a Simulink Project from a MATLAB startup.m file at MATLAB startup can hang MATLAB.
Description: If a startup.m script is executed during MATLAB initialization and uses the Simulink Project API to load a project, MATLAB hangs.Following is an example of using the Simulink Project API to load a project:project = Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.CurrentProject();project.load('c:\projects\myproject');
Workaround: To avoid this problem, move any code which loads a Simulink Project out of your startup.m script which runs at startup.Instead you can access recent projects from Simulink Project's Open menu. Select from the list to load a recent project.

Summary: When nonexistent destination directory is specified, the COPYFILE function copies to a file
Description: When using the COPYFILE or MOVEFILE function and a nonexistent destination directory, denoted by a trailing filesep, is specified, the function ignores the filesep and copies/moves the source file to a file. For example: src = 'myfile.ext'; dst = ['aNonexistentDir', filesep]; copyfile(src, dst); Here, myfile.ext is copied to the file aNonexistentDir. This should have produced an error.
Workaround: Use the EXIST and MKDIR functions to ensure that the destination directory exists before attempting to copy or move the file.

Summary: Images can fail to appear in PDF reports
Description: Images can fail to appear in PDF reports. This can occur if the report's name contains one or more spaces.
Workaround: Rename the report with a name that does not contain spaces.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Constant sample time triggered subsystems might generate incorrect code
Description: A subsystem might generate incorrect code if all of the following are true: The subsystem is a triggered or an enabled and triggered subsystem.  The subsystem has a constant sample time. At least one of the output ports is connected to a signal with a testpoint, or a signal with a non-Auto  signal storage class.
Workaround: A constant sample time triggered subsystem is never triggered. Therefore, one possible workaround is to remove this subsystem.

Summary: Generated code for exported bus type mapped to auto-generated struct type does not compile
Description: If a model contains a bus object and a structure parameter that matches the shape of that bus object, the type definition of the bus might map to the type definition of the structure parameter. If the Data scope  parameter of the bus object is set to Exported , the generated code does not compile.
Workaround: Set the Data scope parameter of the bus object to Auto.

Summary: "Simulink Design Verifier failed to initialize: Checking compatibility failed" error for models containing dynamic lookup tables with fixed point data types
Description: Simulink Design Verifier gives the following error message for models containing Lookup Table Dynamic  blocks with signals of fixed point data type: Simulink Design Verifier failed to initialize: Checking compatibility failed

Summary: Discrete Integrator block not have the needSatDwork in code gen when limits are outside the output datatype range
Description: Discrete Integrator block has an optimization to saturate the output on upper/lower limits. The generated code will be different when the optimization is on/off. When the limit values are outside the range of the output datatype, whether the saturation is turned on or off does not affect the numeric results, because the output will not exceed the limits at all. However the generated code should still have a needSatDwork to show the optimization is on.

Summary: Configuration Parameters dialog box might not be visible
Description: When you open the Configuration Parameters dialog box for the first time, the dialog box might be behind the model and not visible.

Summary: Incorrect scaling in ellipsoidal form of polar stereographic projection, including UPS
Description: The formulation of the Stereographic Azimuthal Projection on the ellipsoid includes a curvature adjustment factor used to maintain the requested scale at the projection origin. This adjustment depends on the latitude of the projection origin. The curvature adjustment factor is computed incorrectly when the following are true: The projection is applied to an ellipsoid (with a nonzero eccentricity) with a projection origin having nonzero latitude (an oblique or polar aspect, as opposed to an equatorial aspect). Data are projected on a map axes with its MapProjection property set to 'stereo'  or 'ups' ˇŞUniversal Polar Stereographic (UPS), or either mfwdtran  or minvtran  is applied to an mstruct with its mapprojection  field set to either of these values.   This bug does not affect the projfwd  or projinv  functions. For a typical reference ellipsoid, the curvature adjustment factor is off by a factor ranging from 1 (no error, that is) at the equator to about 0.993 at either pole. The error of about 7 parts in 1000 at the pole is significant. It might be hard to discern in a small scale map display, but it can easily result in displacements of 10 kilometers or more when converting between latitude and longitude and numerical northing and easting values.
Workaround: If a map display is not required, a straightforward workaround is to use projfwd or projinv in place of mfwdtran or minvtran.The alternative is a downloadable workaround for releases R2012a and R2012b. The bug exists in prior releases as well, but the workaround relies on features added in R2012a.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Download the attached file, workaround_838188-12a_12b_2013-04-30.zip, to a folder of your choosing.On Windows double-click the file to view its contents, then click the Extract button, and extract the contents to a folder of your choosing. On Macintosh, double-click the file to extract its contents. On Linux use the unzip command.Complete the instructions in the enclosed text file, README.txt.

Summary: Signal Builder block Signal > Replace Signal With > Sampled Gaussian Noise dialog box displays the label Variance instead of Standard Deviation
Description: The Signal Builder block Signal > Replace Signal With > Sampled Gaussian Noise  dialog box has a parameter labeled Variance . However, the block interprets this parameter value as the standard deviation. This parameter is improperly labeled on the dialog box.
Workaround: As a workaround, enter the expected standard deviation in the Variance parameter. The block will calculate the sampled Gaussian noise value with the standard deviation value.

Summary: Cancel button fails to cancel retrieving a project from source control
Description: When retrieving a project from source control, there are two buttons enabled. Stop in the source control bar, and Cancel in the project retriever dialog.If you click Stop, this cancels the source control checkout of the project files, which is the correct behaviour.If you click Cancel, this closes the project retrieval dialog, but does not stop the checkout process. The checked out project opens after this process has completed. This behaviour is incorrect.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, click the Stop button instead of the Cancel button.
