Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Multirate model that contains a function call to a Model block
Description: A multirate model that contains a function call to a Model block might generate incorrect code. The code generation process might delete the function body for either the output or subrate step function. This incorrect code generation behavior might happen sporadically.
Workaround: Place the Model block called by the multirate model into a subsystem.

Summary: EtherCAT in xPC Target does not work correctly with the newest TwinCAT or ET9000 configuration tools
Description: The newest TwinCAT and ET9000 EtherCAT configuration tools are not fully compatible with the xPC Target implementation up through R2012b. If distributed clocks are turned on in the configuration,  variable offsets are not handled correctly and communication does not happen correctly. The older ET9000 (v2.11 build 2001) produces compatible configuration files.
Workaround: Download the update for R2011b, R2012a or for R2012b. The update is not compatible with older versions of Matlab.History: First patch release July 31, 2012Second patch release August 21, 2012 to remove some extraneous debug printoutsThird patch release December 10, 2012. The R2012b patch didn't contain the pcode for the dialog boxes.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.After installing the zip file, delete the .slprj directory, the modelname_xpc_rtw directory and then rebuild the model.  The model may not get completely rebuilt if you don't remove these generated directories.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink generates incorrect code when a block requiring elapsed time is inside a For Each subsystem nested within a Triggered subsystem
Description: Simulink generates incorrect code for a model if all  of the following conditions are true: The model contains a For Each subsystem that is inside a Triggered subsystem. One of the following blocks is inside this For Each subsystem: Discrete-Time Integrator block C-mex S-Function block that calls ssGetElapseTime  or ssGetElapseTimeCounter  Model block that references a model containing either of the above two blocks.  None of these blocks are inside any child Triggered subsystem of this For Each subsystem. Parameter Configuration Parameters -> Diagnostics -> Data Validity -> Underspecified initialization detection  is set to Classic .  To check whether this issue affects your model, run the DetectForBugReport833462.p  script. See the Workaround  section below for details. ?
Workaround: Download and unzip the attached attachment_833462_R11b_R12a_20120731.zip script to a folder and add the folder to the MATLAB path using the addpath command. Run the script on your model, using the following syntax:ret = DetectForBugReport833462('modelname')For a model that is unaffected by this issue, the script returns an empty array. If this issue may impact your model, the script returns an array of structures with the following fields:ForEachSubsystem: The For Each subsystem that has trigger sample time and contains blocks using or potentially using elapsed time.DiscreteIntegrator: Discrete-Time Integrator blocks contained in the For Each subsystem.SFunction: C-mex S-Function blocks contained in the For Each subsystem. The script does not check if S-function blocks call ssGetElapseTime or ssGetElapseTimeCounter.ModelReference: Model blocks contained in the For Each subsystem. The script does not check if reference models contain a Discrete Integrator or S-Function block that can trigger this issue.After running the script, check whether the S-function blocks and model reference blocks reported in the result satisfy the conditions described in the "Bug Description" section.To address this issue, use one of these approaches:Set the Configuration Parameters -> Diagnostics -> Data Validity -> Underspecified initialization detection parameter to Simplified orUse an Enable subsystem to provide elapsed time for blocks inside For Each subsystem:Place an Enabled subsystem in the For Each subsystem identified based on the description in the "Bug Description" section.Move all blocks to the Enabled subsystem, except for the For Each, Inport, and Outport blocks.Connect Inport blocks with the Enabled subsystem inputs and Outport blocks with the Enabled subsystem outputs.Place a Constant block in the For Each subsystem and connect the Constant block output to the enable port of Enable subsystem.Double-click the Constant block and set the Constant value parameter to 1.

Summary: Simulink Code Inspector might fail to verify code generated from a compatible reference model
Description: Simulink Code Inspector might fail to verify code generated from a reference model that passes the compatibility checks. You might encounter this issue if code generated for your model propogates attributes differently for top and reference models.  When running the compatibility checks, the Simulink Code Inspector compiles the model as a top model.  During code generation and inspection, you can choose to compile the model as a reference model.  Some model attributes are compatible with code inspection when code is generated as top model, but incompatible when code is generated as a reference model.   In such cases, the model can pass the compatibility checks, even though the model is not compatible when compiled to generate and inspect code as a reference model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Function-call initiator blocks may sort incorrectly when corresponding function-call blocks are connected to a Merge block
Description: Two (or more) function-call initiators may not sort in accordance to their inherent data dependencies when all  of the following conditions are met: The initiators are inside a non-virtual subsystem and their corresponding function-call blocks (i.e., subsystems or models) are outside that non-virtual subsystem. The output of one of the function-call blocks is connected to input of the other function-call block via a Merge block.  This bug may result in either incorrect simulation results (as illustrated in the attached model mG832900_Example1.slx) , or a failure to detect a data dependency violation error (as illustrated in attached model mG832900_Example2.slx ).
Workaround: As a workaround, to get the correct behavior, try not to place initiators inside the non-virtual subsystem. For example, consider changing Subsystem to a virtual subsystem in attached example model mG832900_Example1.slx. Alternatively, use a single common initiator to execute both function-call blocks. For example, consider replacing FcnCallGen1 and FcnCallGen2 by a single initiator block with two function-call outputs in mG832900_Example2.slx.

Summary: Setting the port number of an output event to a different number can fail or cause an error.
Description: In the presence of both output events and output data, changing the port number of an output event can sometimes fail silently, or throw an error. The error message has this text: failed assertion in .\fsm\instance.cpp at line 1383: (*pp)->portNum == index || ((*pp)->portNum == (index+1) && oldIndex < newIndex)
Workaround: You can use the attached script changeEventPortNum.m to programatically set the port number of the event. After downloading the attached zip file, unzip it and add the directory to which you have extracted it to the MATLAB path. Then on the MATLAB prompt: >> help changeEventPortNum to get usage information.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Constant (Inf) sample time subsystem might execute in the wrong order
Description: The generated code for a constant (Inf) sample time subsystem might not execute in the correct order in the parent system initialization function. The constant sample time subsystem code might execute in an incorrect order with respect to constant (Inf) sample time signals with custom storage classes that also have code in the parent system initialization function.
Workaround: Subsystems execute in the correct order relative to other subsystems. Place constant sample time blocks in a parent model into their own atomic, inlined subsystem.

Summary: No compilation warning for missing header file
Description: If Polyspace does not find a header file that is specified in your C++ source code by a #include  directive, your verification might continue without generating a compilation warning in the log file. However, the verification might stop later, producing an error with the following message: identifier is undefined
Workaround: Use the -I option to specify the path to the missing header file.

Summary: Timer block of powerlib_extras library does not handle negative time
Description: The Timer block always assumes the start time of the simulation is 0. If a negative value is specified in the Time  parameter of the block, the following error message is displayed at the start of the simulation:  All transition times must be in increasing order.

Summary: slvnvlogsignals reports 'TestCases' doesn't exist error
Description: If your model contains Model blocks referencing other models and the Simulink Design Verifier analysis mode is set to Property Proving or Design Error Detection, invoking slvnvlogsignals  for your model can result in the following error: Error using slvnvlogsignals (line 40) A field named 'TestCases' doesn't exist.
Workaround: Before you invoke slvnvlogsignals on your model, in the Simulink Editor, go to Analysis > Design Verifier > Options and change the Mode setting to Test Generation.

Summary: Sporadic access violation crashes in libmx.dll with deployed components
Description: Deployed components created from MATLAB Compiler sporadically crash with an access violation, with libmx.dll  as the faulting module.
Workaround: Set the MWDISABLE_MVM_API environment variable to 1 on the machine where the application runs and rerun the application.

Summary: MATLAB might crash or hang during an External mode simulation on an Arduino target
Description: If you run an External mode simulation using an Arduino target, the MATLAB session might crash or appear to hang. The issue occurs when the target sends an incorrect packet to the host.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Change log:November 26, 2012: attachment_828547_12a_2012-11-26.zip: initial version, works on R2012a onlyNovember 26, 2012: attachment_828547_12b_2012-11-26.zip: initial version, works on R2012b only

Summary: An indexing expression in a nested parfor loop may reference the wrong array element
Description: An indexing expression in a nested parfor  loop may reference the wrong element in an array.  A nested parfor  loop and a non-uniform use of indexing on a sliced variable must occur. For example,  the following code produces an incorrect result.a = [1 2 3;4 5 6;7 8 9];b = [0 0 0;0 0 0;0 0 0];parfor i=1:1     for j=2:2         b(i,j) = a(i+1, j) + a(i, j);     endend bb =     0     6     0     0     0     0     0     0     0 The answer should contain a 7, not a 6.
Workaround: Insert a reference to the matrix being indexed outside the for loop but within the parfor loop. For example, the following code produces the correct result.parfor i=1:1    a;    for j=2:2        b(i,j) = a(i+1, j) + a(i, j);    endendbb =     0     7     0     0     0     0     0     0     0

Summary: Opening the dialog box for Bus Creator connected to Variant Subsystem signal can result in an error
Description: Opening the Block Parameters dialog box for a Bus Creator block can result in an error if both  of these conditions exist: One or more of the signals feeding the Bus Creator block originate from Variant Subsystem block The corresponding Simulink.Variant  object is unspecified or unavailable.   The error is usually of the form:  Error occurred when trying to create dialog Error due to multiple causes.
Workaround: Ensure that the Simulink.Variant object is present in the base workspace before opening the Bus Creator block.

Summary: During a debugging session, running a loop that contains a function calling drawnow can increase memory usage after each iteration.
Description: During a debugging session, running a loop that contains a function calling drawnow  can increase memory usage after each iteration. For example, hitting a breakpoint in a script or function and then continuing into such a loop uses and does not release memory on each loop iteration. After many iterations, running the loop causes an out of memory condition. On Windows systems, each loop iteration also uses up one system handle, which can lead to running out of those resources. MATLAB might diagnose the out of memory condition, or might crash or hang. Please note: This problem only occurs if one or more breakpoints are set before the loop, not if there are breakpoints in the loop itself. If the loop ends before running out of memory, MATLAB reclaims the memory.  Example of a case that causes this bug: function cause_memory_loss keyboard % After stopping here, press F5 to continue. timeoutMinutes = 2;   t0 = tic;   while toc(t0) < timeoutMinutes*60       function_calls_drawnow();   end function function_calls_drawnow()   drawnow;
Workaround: The attached work-around can be used with MATLAB versions 2011a and later.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: For Each Subsystem or Pack Boolean data into bitfields might introduce unused global variable
Description: The generated code might have an unused global variable if all of the following conditions are met: In the global structure, an embedded global variable, which is created by either a For Each Subsystem or the selected configuration parameter Pack Boolean data into bitfields , is not in a true global state. All references of the global variable are localized in the generated code.  A variant of this issue is the generated code has an unused empty nested structure, which causes uncompilable code.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect initialization code may be generated when a Stateflow chart is connected to a conditional subsystem's Outport block?
Description: Incorrect code is generated when all  of the following conditions are true: The model has a conditional subsystem and one of the Outport blocks of that subsystem is connected to the data output of a Stateflow chart inside this subsystem. The Initial output  parameter of this Outport block differs from the Initial value  parameter of the data output of the Stateflow chart. The parameter Model Configuration -> Diagnostics -> Data Validity -> Underspecified initialization detection  is set to Classic .  See the attached model, for which all of the above conditions are true. Simulating the model produces the following output: >> yout.signals.values  ans =  2  When you build the code for the model and run the generated executable, you see: >> rt_yout.signals.values  ans =  1  When the generated code is used, you see an erroneous output of 1. ?
Workaround: To address this issue, use any one of the following approaches:1. Set the Configuration Parameters -> Diagnostics -> Data Validity -> Underspecified initialization detection parameter to Simplified. To upgrade your model to simplified initialization mode, MathWorks recommends carrying out the Check consistency of initialization parameters for Outport and Merge blocks check in the Model Advisor.2. Set the Initial value of the data output of the Stateflow chart to match the Initial output of the Outport block.  Note: You cannot use this approach when the output of the Stateflow Chart is a bus signal. The initial value is zero by default, and you cannot modify it.3. Set the Initial output parameter of the Outport block to '[]'.4. Set the Initial output parameter of the Outport block to be the same as the Initial value parameter of the data output of the Stateflow chart.

Summary: Certain HDF files can crash MATLAB? when read as HDF-EOS
Description: Creation of an HDF file creates an internal Vgroup that stores the directory or folder location of the file. If the name of this location has a length exceeding 80 characters, the HDF-EOS library may cause a segmentation fault.

Summary: Potential security vulnerability in FlexNet Publisher lmgrd license server managers as well as MLM vendor daemon.
Description: A possible security vulnerability has been reported in the FlexNet Publisher lmgrd  license server managers as well as vendor daemons. More specifically, it is possible that a malicious user with full access to the internal network could remotely execute arbitrary code under the user context.

Summary: Simulink.Bus, Simulink.Signal, and Simulink.Parameter objects lose their value when passed into a parfor loop
Description: When using Simulink.Bus , Simulink.Signal , or Simulink.Parameter  objects in a parfor  loop, their values are lost. A typical situation in which this may occur is when these objects and the models they are intended to be utilized with are inside a parfor  loop. In this situation, the objects are converted to doubles. ?

Summary: Messages about Time Machine when launching MATLAB on Mac OS 10.7 (Lion)
Description: When running MATLAB from a Terminal window on Mac OS X 10.7 (Lion), the following message is displayed multiple times:"This process is attempting to exclude an item from Time Machine by path without administrator privileges."This message is generated by a third party library and can safely be ignored.

Summary: Configuration Parameters dialog crashes when switching between system target files with same 'Category' name
Description: If you derive two custom targets from tornado.tlc  or ert.tlc  and both use the same 'Category'  name in the rtwoptions  structure, when you switch between the two targets, the Configuration Parameters dialog box crashes.
Workaround: In the definition of the System Target Files (STF), specify a unique 'Category' name.For example, if you are defining two STF, target1.tlc and target2.tlc, which are both derived from tornado.tlc, specify the 'Category' name for target1.tlc as 'foo1':  rtwoptions(index).prompt        = 'foo1';  rtwoptions(index).type          = 'Category';  rtwoptions(index).enable        = 'on';    rtwoptions(index).popupstrings  = '';  rtwoptions(index).tlcvariable   = '';  rtwoptions(index).tooltip       = '';  rtwoptions(index).callback      = '';  rtwoptions(index).opencallback  = '';  rtwoptions(index).closecallback = '';  rtwoptions(index).makevariable  = '';  index = index + 1;and in target2.tlc use a different name, 'foo2', for the 'Category' field:  rtwoptions(index).prompt        = 'foo2';  rtwoptions(index).type          = 'Category';  rtwoptions(index).enable        = 'on';    rtwoptions(index).popupstrings  = '';  rtwoptions(index).tlcvariable   = '';  rtwoptions(index).tooltip       = '';  rtwoptions(index).callback      = '';  rtwoptions(index).opencallback  = '';  rtwoptions(index).closecallback = '';  rtwoptions(index).makevariable  = '';  index = index + 1;

Summary: Constant value block input sources can result in incorrect code.
Description: When the code generator reads constant data, the generator can access memory outside of the constant data storage. As a result, the generator may define constant values incorrectly in the generated code. This error is more likely to occur if the embedded target has no built-in 8 or 16-bit integer data types. The integer types are shown in the Configuration Parameters dialog box on the Hardware Implementation  pane. The problem has been observed on a 64-bit Windows host. ?
Workaround: Use 32-bit data types or a data type that is built-in to the hardware for constants or ground values.

Summary: datenum and datevec functions error unexpectedly on 64-bit Windows systems
Description: Calling the datenum  or datevec  functions with date strings as input arguments sometimes errors unpredictably. For example, datenum('20050414','yyyymmdd')  returns ??? Error using ==> datenum at 174   DATENUM failed. Caused by:       Error using ==> dtstr2dtnummx       Failed on converting date string to date number.  However, calling the same function a second time with the same inputs does not error. This error does not depend on the actual input values.
Workaround: Restarting MATLAB will solve the issue temporarily, but the error may return.You can put the line of MATLAB code that calls datenum or datevec in a try-catch block, where the catch block repeats the first call, and the second call will succeed. For example,try datenums = datenum(dateStrs,format);catch datenums = datenum(dateStrs,format);endAlternatively, you might prevent the error by specifying a pivot year, even when it is not needed. For example,datenums = datenum(dateStrs,format,1960);Installation instructions       Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. Summary: Java exception when printing from Figure window File menu on a non-default printer in MATLAB 7.9 (R2009b) on Redhat Linux
Description: If you set the PRINTER  environment variable in the environment variable script (.cshrc ) to use a non-default printer, for example, setenv PRINTER hp8000dn-1 and you then print from a figure File  menu, the print dialog does not display. Instead, the following exception occurs: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: services must contain defaultServiceat javax.print.ServiceUI.printDialog(Unknown Source)at com.mathworks.page.export.printdlg.Printdlg.setVisible(Printdlg.java:470)at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method)at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(Unknown Source)at sun.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(Unknown Source)at java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Unknown Source)at com.mathworks.jmi.AWTUtilities$Invoker$3.runWithOutput(AWTUtilities.java:492)at com.mathworks.jmi.AWTUtilities$Invoker$2.watchedRun(AWTUtilities.java:446)at com.mathworks.jmi.AWTUtilities$WatchedRunnable.run(AWTUtilities.java:409)at java.awt.event.InvocationEvent.dispatch(Unknown Source)at java.awt.EventQueue.dispatchEvent(Unknown Source)at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.pumpOneEventForFilters(Unknown Source)at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.pumpEventsForFilter(Unknown Source)at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.pumpEventsForHierarchy(Unknown Source)at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.pumpEvents(Unknown Source)at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.pumpEvents(Unknown Source)at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.run(Unknown Source) ?
Workaround: Specify PRINTER to point to the default network printer.

Summary: The title of a UIPanel does not resize when the GUI is resized
Description: When a user resizes a GUI which contains a UIPanel, the size of the panel's title remains the same even when its FontUnits  property is set to 'normalized' .

Summary: Sensitivities might be incorrect if any species are in concentration.
Description: If any species are in concentration, the reported sensitivities might be incorrect. If the error occurs, a particular sensitivity will be off by a constant multiplicative factor over the course of the simulation. In R2012a and earlier, the bug might occur when you perform sensitivity analysis with full normalization and: The SpeciesInputFactors  property (R2011a and earlier) or the Inputs  property (R2011b and later) contains a species, say x , which is specified in concentration. Either UnitConversion  is true , or UnitConversion  is false  and  species x  is in a compartment whose capacity is not equal to 1.0.  In R2012b, the bug might occur under the above conditions or if any of the following are true: the model contains a repeated assignment rule that modifies a species; or a species is not modified by any reactions or rules.
Workaround: In R2012a and earlier, set Normalization to 'None' or 'Half' rather than 'Full'. If the fully normalized sensitivites are required, perform the normalization manually by scaling the sensitivites appropriately.In R2012b, install the attachment for R2012b of MATLAB by following the installation instructions below.Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Determine the architecture of this MATLAB installation by typing computer('arch') at the MATLAB command prompt.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached architecture-specific .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Some font sizes cause the text?function with the LaTeX interpreter to produce text that extends beyond the right edge of the background rectangle
Description: Create a text-object with the function text  using the following command:text('String','FontSize(20),... ????  'Interpreter','latex',... ????  'Position',[0.1,0.3],...      'EdgeColor','k',...      'Backgroundcolor',[1,1,0.5]); The text extends beyond the right side of the background rectangle.
Workaround: The best font sizes to use are those closest to the design size of the fonts within the text string.? The default LaTeX font size is 10, so setting a 'FontSize' close to 10 will work best for this example.? It the text string refers to a 17 point font, e.g., '\font\myfont=cmr17 \myfont This is a test string', then setting 'FontSize' close to 17 would be ideal.
